label: January 1
what_now_1: Happy New Year! Click Share if today marks the start of your year long journey of reading through the Bible, a spiritual journey that will impact the way you live your life. Share this post with your friends and encourage them to read through the Bible together! Ready? Lets GO! Heb 12:1 (GNT) '... let us rid ourselves of everything that gets in the way, and of the sin which holds on to us so tightly, and let us run with determination the race that lies before us.'
what_now_2: In the beginning God...

label: January 2
what_now_1: Do you see the impact of the fall in the world today? How is Satan attempting to use the same methods of deceit described in Genesis 3 in your own life today?
what_now_2: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction. ~ Proverbs 1:7 (KJV)

label: January 3
what_now_1: In what ways does our society today resemble that of Noah's time?
what_now_2: If God were looking for one righteous person today, would He look to you? How can you adjust your actions and/or attitudes to reflect God's righteousness?

label: January 4
what_now_1: When Jesus calls out 'Come, follow me' in Matthew 4:18-22, the disciples are quick to respond. Simon and Andrew leave their nets 'at once' to follow him, James and John also follow him immediately, 'leaving the boat and their father behind.' How do you respond when you sense the prompting of the Spirit? Do you drop your nets and follow or do you make excuses?
what_now_2: The Psalmist often cries out to God in frustration and desperation. Do your prayers reveal that level of openness and honesty?

label: January 5
what_now_1: Praise God for the moments in your life when he has blessed your path because you heeded his directions.
what_now_2: Is God calling you to take a leap of faith like Abram?

label: January 6
what_now_1: Does Jesus' teaching in the Sermon on the Mount convict you for living by the letter of the law (thou shall not kill) and ignoring the Spirit of the Law (thou shall not hate)? What steps can you take to better embrace the Spirit of the Law?
what_now_2: Consider Abram's honesty with God in Genesis 15, is your relationship with God such that you can honestly share your heart?

label: January 7
what_now_1: Today marks our first week thru the Bible this year. If you've missed any readings do not be discouraged, know this is common for many people. The important thing is to be in God's word, not to be a slave to the schedule. So if you are behind, read today's readings and be encouraged. - Phil 3:12 (CEV) - I have not yet reached my goal, and I am not perfect. But Christ has taken hold of me. So I keep on running...
what_now_2: Abram trusted Sarai's plan over God's long-range plan. In what areas are you tempted to short-cut God's plans for your life?

label: January 8
what_now_1: Abraham's interaction with God concerning Sodom and Gomorrah points to the value of praying for others. Who needs your intercessional prayer today?
what_now_2: 'So don't worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will bring its own worries. Today's trouble is enough for today.' ~ Matthew 6:34 (NLT) Biblical Truth: It serves no purpose to be preoccupied with what might happen, so don't worry about tomorrow; let God be God in your life today.

label: January 9
what_now_1: Again, Abraham lies about Sarah being his sister. This story reveals the tendency of people toward specific sins. Is there a temptation that snares you most regularly? Ask for God's forgiveness and assistance in avoiding those temptations.
what_now_2: When we are willing to surrender that which is most dear to us to serve our Heavenly Father we demonstrate our love and worship to Him.

label: January 10
what_now_1: The love story of Isaac and Rebekah is even more powerful when we consider the hand of God working to bring the two together through the prayer of Abraham's servant. Celebrate God's providential work in bringing you into your current situation.
what_now_2: Trust in the Lord with all your heart; do not depend on your own understanding. Seek his will in all you do, and he will show you which path to take. ~ Proverbs 3:5-6 NLT

label: January 11
what_now_1: Christ answers the prayers of those that call Him Lord. He is able to calm our fear and strengthen our faith in the most turbulent circumstance.
what_now_2: Some offered Jesus excuses while others followed. Are there excuses holding you back from fully following Christ in faith?

label: January 12
what_now_1: The patriarchs often built altars to commemorate God moments in their life, what God moments do you need to commemorate?
what_now_2: Jesus asked, 'Why do you entertain evil thoughts in your hearts?' (Matthew 9:4) Are you convicted by this question?

label: January 13
what_now_1: Pray that God would raise up workers for the harvest in your city.
what_now_2: 'The girl is not dead, but asleep.' (Matthew 9:24) Jesus calls you to believe the unbelievable... to trust Him when life is at its worst... Are you having trouble believing the unbelievable in your life?

label: January 14
what_now_1: After Jesus asked His disciples to pray for workers for the harvest, He sends them to the harvest field. Where has Jesus sent you? How are you harvesting in your sphere of influence: your job, your school, your family, etc.?
what_now_2: Wisdom is more valuable than jewels; nothing you could want can compare with it. ~ Proverbs 3:15 GNT

label: January 15
what_now_1: Whoever acknowledges me before others, I will also acknowledge before my Father in heaven. But whoever disowns me before others, I will disown before my Father in heaven. ~ Matthew 10:32-33 (NIV)
what_now_2: No matter what man's opinion of us is, we are valuable in the sight of God, who can silence all fears | Source: Fearful to Fearless.

label: January 16
what_now_1: El Elohe Israel - Mighty is the God of Israel! When you are weary remember, the God of Israel is your Mighty God, too!
what_now_2: How are your wrestling skills? Jacob would not let go of God until he received a blessing. Are you willing to hold on to God with that sort of fervor? Do you have any scars, like Jacob's limp, from holding on to God? Share the story of your scars with someone today who needs encouragement in their faith.

label: January 17
what_now_1: In psalm 15 the Psalmist describes several characteristics of those whose walk is blameless. Use those characteristics as a self-test. (Are you righteous? Do you speak the truth? Do you slander? etc.) In those areas where your actions are not blameless ask for God's forgiveness and help.
what_now_2: Wisdom guides us through life, keeps us from temptation so that we can walk in confidence and without fear. We are protected by the one that never sleeps.

label: January 18
what_now_1: Jesus condemns the Pharisees for their lack of fruit and warns that man will be held accountable for every word they speak. How will your words stand up to the scrutiny of God's judgment?
what_now_2: Praise God today for the moments that he has been your refuge.

label: January 19
what_now_1: Are you cultivating the seeds that God places in your life?
what_now_2: Joseph languished in prison for two more years due to the forgetfulness of the cupbearer. Are you currently waiting on God? Remember Joseph's example of patience in the prison and more importantly, remember God's faithfulness!

label: January 20
what_now_1: Joseph's brothers display a growing guilt in this week's readings. Are you being tormented with guilt?
what_now_2: A small amount of faith can develop beyond all expectation. How is God stretching your faith?

label: January 21
what_now_1: Once we are caught in the shadow of guilt we can only escape it through the blood of Christ! Do you have unconfessed sin you need to bring to God today?
what_now_2: Does your life reflect the pursuit of wisdom? What can you do to grow in wisdom?

label: January 22
what_now_1: Joseph forgave, in the power of God's mercy. Is there someone desperately longing for your forgiveness today?
what_now_2: But Jesus spoke to them at once. Don't be afraid, he said. Take courage. I am here! ~ Matthew 14:26-27 (NLT) --- The more we know Christ the more we trust Him. The storms of fear are silenced because Christ is near.

label: January 23
what_now_1: 'May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be pleasing in your sight, O Lord, my Rock and my Redeemer.' (Psalm 19:14) Ask the Holy Spirit to watch your words and thoughts closely and reveal those that are not pleasing to the Father.
what_now_2: Joseph is reunited with his father. The New Testament speaks of a day of joyous reunion as well, the day of Christ's return.

label: January 24
what_now_1: Jesus repeatedly says to the disciples, 'You of little faith.' (Matthew 16:8) How would Jesus describe your faith?
what_now_2: Jacob blesses his sons... fathers and grandfathers... how can you bestow a blessing on your descendants today?

label: January 25
what_now_1: What a comfort it is that Christ is nearby, that through His word and touch of grace He is able to remove the clouds of fear. -- While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, 'This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!' When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid that they fell flat on the ground. But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, 'Get up and don't be afraid!' ~ Matthew 17:5-7 (CEV)
what_now_2: Are you willing to die to self each day and give up everything for Jesus? What is He calling you to surrender today? -- 'Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me. For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.' ~ Matthew 16:24-25 (KJV)

label: January 26
what_now_1: Today's selection from the book of Proverbs begs the question; 'Is it worth it?' Are the things that command your time, talent and energy really worth it? -- The Message paraphrases it like this: You don't want to end your life full of regrets, nothing but sin and bones, Saying, 'Oh, why didn't I do what they told me? Why did I reject a disciplined life? Why didn't I listen to my mentors, or take my teachers seriously? My life is ruined! I haven't one blessed thing to show for my life!'
what_now_2: How have you seen mountains move as a result of faith the size of a mustard seed?

label: January 27
what_now_1: When Moses made excuses to God, God said 'What is that in your hand?' -- It's easy to list the things we cannot do for God, but the better question is What has He given you that you can and must use for His Kingdom? -- Now Go! Pick up your staff! And know that God is with you!
what_now_2: Then he said, I tell you the truth, unless you turn from your sins and become like little children, you will never get into the Kingdom of Heaven. So anyone who becomes as humble as this little child is the greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven. ~ Matthew 18:3-4 NLT

label: January 28
what_now_1: The parable of the unmerciful servant reveals someone that has forgotten the grace they received. Give thanks to God for mercies He has shown you and you may find it easier to display mercy to others.
what_now_2: I may walk through valleys as dark as death, but I won't be afraid. You are with me, and your shepherd's rod makes me feel safe. ~ Psalm 23:4 (CEV) - Biblical Truth: Christ is our shepherd providing comfort to His flock, He protects and guides us through the darkest times. We have nothing to fear because nothing can separate us from God?s love. #fearnot

label: January 29
what_now_1: God protected the land of Goshen where the Israelites resided. Spend time recalling the times you have experienced His protection... take a moment to share one of them with a friend or co-worker.
what_now_2: The earth and all within it are the property of God. Praise him for His dominion over all things.

label: January 30
what_now_1: Pharaoh waivers back and forth between submission and arrogance. This same battle rages within each of us. Submit yourself to the Lord and repent of the arrogance in your life
what_now_2: The Passover lamb foreshadows the culmination of God's redemptive plan of Christ and the cross; Praise God today for His Passover Lamb!

label: January 31
what_now_1: Proverbs warns against deceit and dissension, pray that God would cleanse your heart of these as both will demolish the church and the witness of the body.
what_now_2: We are completing 1/12 of our journey through the Bible today. Keep your eyes fixed on Jesus and if you've missed some readings don't worry, stay in His Word and spring forward to todays readings. - Encourage your friends by letting them know what being in the Word each day has meant to you! -- 'Let us keep our eyes fixed on Jesus, on whom our faith depends from beginning to end. He did not give up because of the cross! On the contrary, because of the joy that was waiting for him, he thought nothing of the disgrace of dying on the cross, and he is now seated at the right side of God's throne.' ~ Hebrews 12:2 (GNT)

label: February 1
what_now_1: But Moses told the people, 'Don't be afraid. Just stand still and watch the LORD rescue you today. The Egyptians you see today will never be seen again.' ~ Exodus 14:13 (NLT) -- In times of difficulty keep your spirits calm so that you may be prepared to follow God's lead in doing His work... as well as your own.
what_now_2: God did not allow Israel to take the shorter route because He knew they would face battles. Think back and thank God for the times in your life you avoided pitfalls because He took you the long way!

label: February 2
what_now_1: Israel quickly forgets God's gifts and grumbles about their circumstances. Their short memories seem so obvious to us... Have you forgotten blessings and complaining today?
what_now_2: God's provision for Israel might not have been fancy, but it met their needs. Give thanks for God's provision in your life!

label: February 3
what_now_1: Joshua led the ground attack while Moses led the spiritual attack. Are you covering the ground troops with spiritual protection in your prayers? -- Share this post with your facebook friends and ask that they join you in praying for those on the front lines... pastors, missionaries, ministry leaders, etc.
what_now_2: Jesus rebukes the Pharisees for making their faith a public performance. Does your public life reflect your private life? Is your private life a reflection of your public life?

label: February 4
what_now_1: God's laws reveal His character and nature, they also reveal His expectations for righteousness. The Ten Commandments point us toward the Redemption of God in Jesus Christ, the perfect sacrifice for imperfect sinners. Thank God today for His law and His gift!
what_now_2: The Lord is my strength and shield. I trust him with all my heart. He helps me, and my heart is filled with joy. I burst out in songs of thanksgiving. ~ Psalm 28:7 (NLT)

label: February 5
what_now_1: The reading from Proverbs reveals the subtle temptation of seduction. Make a list of the areas you are tempted and pray that God protects your path against them.
what_now_2: This is an amazing video of the Kimyal Tribe of Papua, Indonesia celebrating the arrival of the New Testament Bible in the Kimyal language. -- Do you treasure His word like the Kimyal tribe does? 'And the Good News about the Kingdom will be preached throughout the whole world, so that all nations will hear it, and then the end will come' ~ Matthew 24:14 (NLT)

label: February 6
what_now_1: Praise God for the moments that He has lifted you from the depths.
what_now_2: Are you being a good steward of what God has given you as you await His return? How can you use your time, talent and treasure to honor Him?

label: February 7
what_now_1: The parable of the virgins is a reminder for us to be prepared for the return of Christ. Does your life display preparedness? Are there things you can do in order to keep the imminent return of Christ on your consciousness?
what_now_2: His lord said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant; you were faithful over a few things, I will make you ruler over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.' -- The parable of the talents in todays reading of Matthew 25 reminds me of the Paul Baloche song titled 'My Reward'

label: February 8
what_now_1: What could you do for 'the least of these'?
what_now_2: Are you in distress like the psalmist? Pray today's reading from Psalm 31; if not... pray it for someone you know who is hurting.

label: February 9
what_now_1: Scholars have many theories on why Judas betrayed Jesus. Some assume that Judas was God's pawn, others think Judas was frustrated because Jesus would not lead an earthly revolution, still others believe Judas was upset about the use of funds. The latter options indicate that Jesus wasn't who Judas expected. Do you betray Jesus when your expectations of Him differ from His revelation of Himself?
what_now_2: Notice the connection between the blood of the old Testament sacrifice displayed in Aaron's purification with the words Jesus used to introduce the Lord's Supper. 'This is my blood of the covenant.' Give thanks for the blood that Jesus shed that purifies us from sin!

label: February 10
what_now_1: David records the joy of God's forgiveness in Psalm 32. If your bones are wasting away with guilt, accept the grace God extends. Celebrate his forgiveness today!
what_now_2: Peter drew his sword to face the mob and once again Jesus rebukes him. Sometimes it is difficult to know when to act and when to keep still. Ask God for wisdom and help in recognizing the difference.

label: February 11
what_now_1: Peter goes from drawing his sword to denying His Lord! Consider instances when you made the same slippery slide and give thanks to God for His forgiveness.
what_now_2: The voice that spoke the world into existence silently accepted the punishment for our sins. Praise God that the voice of creation will also welcome you into eternity through the sacrifice of the Son.

label: February 12
what_now_1: Moses descended the mountain with his face glowing with the glory of God! Does your faith walk reflect the glory of God?
what_now_2: Some of the same people who clamored for Christ's execution had shouted His hosannas earlier in the week. How could people change their tune so quickly?

label: February 13
what_now_1: God calls for those skilled in construction to participate in the process of constructing the tabernacle. The community of God needs all the skills, talents and abilities that He has provided for the Body. How are you using your skills and talents for the Kingdom?
what_now_2: It is important to remember that even though Pilate and the Jewish leaders seem to play powerful roles, their roles were directed by the All-Knowing Author! Ask God to remind you of His activity in the world when things seem out of control.v

label: February 14
what_now_1: The passage in Matthew provides our foundation and our mission: 1) Our foundation is an empty tomb that reveals that death is defeated and God is All-Powerful! 2) Mt 28:18-20 reveals the Great Commission to make disciples of our Master!
what_now_2: Then Jesus said to them, 'Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.' ~ Matthew 28:10 (NIV) -- The resurrection of Christ silences fear and fills us with hope and joy. Now go out and proclaim it!

label: February 15
what_now_1: Mark wants his readers to recognize that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the Living God. As you read Mark's gospel, ask God to imprint you with an accurate understanding of Jesus.
what_now_2: The Israelites were completely dependent on God in the desert. They moved when He moved, they stopped when He stopped. Pray that God would help you recognize and practice the same dependence on Him.

label: February 16
what_now_1: As you read Leviticus, look beyond the letter of the law to discover the Spirit of the Law to see what it reveals about God. Understand that no one can live up to the law in its entirety, and that it foreshadows the grace of God that is revealed in Christ. Ask God for a better understanding of grace as you read the book of Leviticus.
what_now_2: Jesus practiced the discipline of spending time alone with God. How can you follow his example?

label: February 17
what_now_1: 'The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.' (Mark 2:27) God desires a relationship over ritual. Is your faith focused on ritual or on a relationship with God?
what_now_2: In their zeal to protect their religion from impurity the Pharisees rejected Jesus. Ironically He was not 'pure' enough for them. Are there ways in which you protect the purity of your religion at the expense of a relationship with Jesus?

label: February 18
what_now_1: Don't give in to worry or anger; it only leads to trouble. Those who trust in the Lord will possess the land, but the wicked will be driven out. ~ Psalm 37:8-9 (GNT) -- When we give in to anger and worry over the prosperity of those who do evil we open ourselves up to temptation. The actions of the wicked are costly and judgment is certain.
what_now_2: Some read Psalm 37:4 and assume that God will give them anything they want. Instead... the words of the Psalmist reveal that God changes our desires to match His desires. Where do you see God actively working to adjust your desires to match His heart?

label: February 19
what_now_1: 'With the measure you use, it will be measured to you' (Mark 4:24) ~ How we treat others has a direct impact on the way God treats us. Based on that revelation, do you need to adjust your treatment of others?
what_now_2: Are there things that are preventing the Word of God from finding fertile ground in your heart? If you are just reading the Bible in order to check an item off your to-do list... STOP NOW! God wants to transform your life through the reading of His Word. Let the Word bear fruit in your life!

label: February 20
what_now_1: Israel was overwhelmed by the presence of God; How is God overwhelming you?
what_now_2: 'Teacher, don't you care if we drown?' (Mark 4:38) The pleas of the disciples rose over the roar of the sea. When have you asked the question, 'Jesus, don't you care if I drown?' Reflect on the ways that Jesus revealed His presence in those times.

label: February 21
what_now_1: Two desperate figures from two different walks of life come to Jesus for healing. Jesus works in both lives without distinction of income or status. God is always more concerned with the heart! Do you allow stereotyping to hamper your response to others?
what_now_2: Proverb 10:8 reminds us of the wisdom of listening. How can you stop the 'chatter' long enough to hear the wisdom others speak into your life?

label: February 22
what_now_1: The grisly story of John's death reveals the character of two men; Herod and John. One stood for righteousness and courageously faced persecution; the other allowed others to determine his decisions and was not willing to repent of his sin. Where do you see more of John in your character? Where do you see more of Herod in your character?
what_now_2: In what ways do you look at life differently in light of the brevity described in Psalm 39?

label: February 23
what_now_1: Jesus offers rest to His disciples. He was concerned for their physical and spiritual well being. Is the pace of your life so hectic that it may be detrimental to your health? Is Jesus calling you to make changes to your schedule and rest?
what_now_2: The Levitical laws addressed mildew in the home. While these details may seem minute, it is important to consider the concern that God had for His people. He wanted the best for them in all areas of their lives. He wants the best for you as well!

label: February 24
what_now_1: Jesus rebuked the Pharisees for allowing the traditions of men to overshadow the laws of God. What traditions or practices do you have that are not grounded in God's Word?
what_now_2: Christ declared that all foods were clean and lifted some of the prohibitions in the Old Testament that we have read recently. It also reveals that God is more concerned with internal transformation than with external rituals.

label: February 25
what_now_1: God specifically instructed His people to set themselves apart from the religious practices of other nations. How are you tempted to allow your worship of God to take on cultural, worldly trappings?
what_now_2: Jesus was willing to physically touch those that He healed. How can you get your hands dirty extending the grace of Christ?

label: February 26
what_now_1: The disciples thought Jesus was talking about physical yeast, but He was making a spiritual analogy. How often do you focus more on the physical world when Jesus is concerned with your spiritual condition?
what_now_2: Who do you say that Jesus is? This is the most important question in life!

label: February 27
what_now_1: God commanded the Israelites to not live by the customs of the land they were about to enter. How are you tempted to live by the customs of this world instead of God's kingdom?
what_now_2: 'I do believe; help me overcome my unbelief!' (Mark 9:24) Can you relate to the father in this story? Ask God to strengthen your faith and deepen your belief.

label: February 28
what_now_1: God decreed that the people must not bring maimed sacrifices... they should bring their very best. Are you giving your best talent, time and treasures to God?
what_now_2: Today we are completing our 2nd month reading through the Bible together! We're at the point in our journey where some people get discouraged and have fallen behind. If that's you please don't worry about reading to 'catch up', but do consider jumping back in with the current readings and know that the most important thing is to be in God's Word! Friends please SHARE THIS POST with your friends to encourage them in their reading. | Heb 12:3 (GNT): 'Think of what he went through; how he put up with so much hatred from sinners! So do not let yourselves become discouraged and give up...'

label: March 1
what_now_1: While we would never stone those who blaspheme God, how do you react when you hear God's name taken in vain?
what_now_2: ...Let the children come to me. Don't stop them! For the Kingdom of God belongs to those who are like these children. I tell you the truth, anyone who doesn't receive the Kingdom of God like a child will never enter it. ~ Mark 10:14-15 -- Ponder the distinction between child-like faith and childish faith.

label: March 2
what_now_1: The disciples continue to see prominence in God's kingdom, are you tempted to seek status over servanthood?
what_now_2: The blind man at Jericho continued to cry out in desperation to Jesus until Jesus healed Him. Once Bartimaeus eyes were opened he made use of his sight... he continued to follow Jesus. It is not enough to come to Christ for spiritual healing, when we are healed we must continue to follow Him.

label: March 3
what_now_1: The priests inspected the donations of the people to judge their quality. Are you giving your best to God or are you just giving Him your leftovers?
what_now_2: Relying on the strength of God, we need not worry about quaking mountains or raging seas. The Psalmist reminds us that God's power is greater than any act of nature.

label: March 4
what_now_1: The Levites were set aside by God for work in the Temple. As believers we have all been set aside to live as His priests. How can you live as a priest in the world?
what_now_2: Are you facing a storm in your life? In the midst of the storm, remember to stand firm in God's strength!

label: March 5
what_now_1: The priests assembling the tabernacle paint a picture of teamwork that we should imitate in the church today. What is your role in the body and how are you fulfilling that role?
what_now_2: The Psalmist concludes today's psalm by praising the eternal nature of God. As you consider the temporal nature of this world, praise God for His eternal existence and dominion!

label: March 6
what_now_1: The teachers of the law flaunted their prominent positions and placed more value in the length of their prayers than in the quality. How would you evaluate your own position and prayers? Are there areas you need to address to be more humble and sincere in your prayer life?
what_now_2: Num 6:24-26 (NLT): May the Lord bless you and protect you. May the Lord smile on you and be gracious to you. May the Lord show you his favor and give you his peace.

label: March 7
what_now_1: Jesus said that even He did not know the time of the end, His warnings about returning should encourage us to be vigilant! In what ways could you live better with the end in mind?
what_now_2: Give praise for those times that God has provided a refuge for you on your journey!

label: March 8
what_now_1: Psalm 51 offers a model of repentance that we can model today to insure a clean slate before God.
what_now_2: Complaining can be contagious, the people began complaining of their hardship to Moses. Are you being infected by the complaints of others? Are you guilty of infecting others with your own complaints? Gratitude is the remedy to stop it in its tracks. Take some time to thank God for your blessings today.

label: March 9
what_now_1: Pentecost reminds us that the spirit of God is in His people! How is God's Spirit at work in you?
what_now_2: Ten spies reported that the land was full of giants, two argued that God would still be victorious! Which group do you most readily associate with?

label: March 10
what_now_1: Israel was on the threshold of the Promised Land yet longed for the chains of Egypt. Are there areas of sin that you long for? In what ways are you enjoying God's Promises?
what_now_2: Peter got it! He declared that Jesus was the Christ... and then denied Him. When have you denied Christ in your words or actions? Take comfort in Christ's forgiveness of Peter.

label: March 11
what_now_1: David writes Psalm 54 while running from Saul, he feels betrayed and abandoned. If you are facing a similar experience let today's Psalm be your prayer today!
what_now_2: Take time to soak in the events of the crucifixion; feel the hammers, see the blood, smell the stench and hear the cries. Meditate on His sacrifice and thank Him for His gift!

label: March 12
what_now_1: God raised His Son from the dead; He is still in the stone removal business. No matter how great the obstacle God is able to move it! What obstacles litter your path today?
what_now_2: Don't dwell on sorrowful times or things of the past, but rejoice and believe in what is ahead. Remember that Christ goes before us to prepare a way.

label: March 13
what_now_1: As we embark on reading Luke's gospel, ask God to continue opening your eyes to the story of His Son.
what_now_2: Israel forgets that God wanted to take them to the Promised Land, they again complain of how Egypt was better than the wilderness. Are you guilty of complaining about the places that your sin has led you? Repent and quit complaining!

label: March 14
what_now_1: The people grew impatient with God. How often do we find ourselves in this predicament? Ask God to forgive impatient times and to seek His strength for more patience in the future.
what_now_2: '...nothing is impossible with God.' (Luke 1:37) This Scripture (Luke 1:37) is often misconstrued. So often we think that God will do anything we want Him to do, but rather it teaches that God can and will accomplish His purposes in all situations. How does God want to do the impossible through you today in order to accomplish His plan?

label: March 15
what_now_1: God used a donkey to steer Balaam from a 'reckless path.' How is God using circumstances in your life to keep you from making destructive choices?
what_now_2: Like Balak, the world will attempt to place words in our mouth. The world will try to make us say false things about God. Ask God to give you strength and courage to speak the Truth!

label: March 16
what_now_1: Balaam turned his back on financial gain and spoke the truth about Israel. No matter the cost or potential loss we must speak God's Truth. Are you tempted to 'shade' the truth in order to profit or keep peace?
what_now_2: God used the census of a pagan ruler to fulfill the prophecy of Christ's birth place. Even though situations appear bleak on the surface God is working to bring about His plans and purpose. What concerns you most about the world today: economics, politics, terrorism, war, etc.? Ask God to help you recognize His activity in the midst of all the struggles.

label: March 17
what_now_1: The genealogies of the Old Testament were important to Israel. Those who have discovered the grace of Christ are a part of Christ's kingdom due to spiritual inheritance! Rejoice that you have a place in God's genealogy!
what_now_2: The Psalmist reminds us that we must make sure that we are on God's side rather than presuming that He is on our side. God doesn't join us... we must join Him!

label: March 18
what_now_1: The Psalmist longed to stand on 'the rock that is higher than I.' (Psalm 61:2) Do you need to stand on that rock today? If you are battered by the world, stand firm on the security of Christ!
what_now_2: John the Baptist introduces the idea that repentance is accompanied by fruit. How are you bearing fruit that confirms your repentance?

label: March 19
what_now_1: Allow God to be your refuge in times of trouble!
what_now_2: The Israelites were instructed to offer sacrifices to observe specific days to remind them of God's activity. Do you have special days or events in your spiritual journey that remind you of His blessings?

label: March 20
what_now_1: People often make commitments to God that they never keep, or intend to keep. The book of Numbers reminds the people of Israel of the significance of an oath before God. Are there things you need to change in order to honor your commitments before God?
what_now_2: Jesus provides a pattern for resisting the temptation of Satan... knowing the Word of God! Do you know Scripture? Do you have it tucked away in your memory so that you can use it against the temptation of Satan? Begin to memorize Scripture today!

label: March 21
what_now_1: Israel's wanderings might seem like dry reading, but each spot on the list represented a place where God revealed Himself to Israel because of a tangible expression of obedience. Take a moment and list some mile markers on your spiritual journey and note how God directed you. It might help you see where He is directing you.
what_now_2: A beautiful woman who lacks discretion is like a gold ring in a pig's snout. ~ Proverbs 11:22 (NLT) -- Today's Proverb reminds us that beauty is far less important than wisdom! How can you grow in wisdom?

label: March 22
what_now_1: God commanded Israel to root out the inhabitants of the land; this was intended to keep Israel free from the hindrance of sin. What inhabitants of the land need to be driven out of your life?
what_now_2: The Psalmist writes of forgiveness and blessing, use the psalm as a guide for your own song of praise to God.

label: March 23
what_now_1: Moses provides a historical review of Israel's wandering to remind the people of God's faithfulness, and to inspire them to be faithful to Him. Take a few moments to retrace your own spiritual journey.
what_now_2: How can you refresh others with your words and deeds today?

label: March 24
what_now_1: Jesus dedicated time to pray before calling the twelve disciples. Do you have an important upcoming decision? Set aside a time to be intentional with God!
what_now_2: Pray that God would help you to love your enemies.

label: March 25
what_now_1: Do you practice self reflection? The Pharisees ignored the log in their own eye while trying to remove the speck from their neighbor's eye. Take care of your own sin before you seek to engage the sin of others.
what_now_2: Are there areas in your life that you are hesitant to submit to the Lordship of Christ?

label: March 26
what_now_1: Moses repeats the Ten Commandments to prepare Israel to take the Promised Land. Take time and review them yourself and ask God to reveal the spirit of each.
what_now_2: John the Baptist needed assurance that Jesus was the Christ. Don't be discouraged when Satan sends doubts your way, Christ is willing to offer hope to those who doubt.

label: March 27
what_now_1: God's discipline is an act of love, not anger. If you are sensing His rebuke, perhaps you should thank Him!
what_now_2: The woman with the perfume made an extravagant gesture of devotion. When was the last time you acted that extravagantly toward Jesus?

label: March 28
what_now_1: God's commands are given for our benefit, ask Him to help you understand them from the proper perspective.
what_now_2: Moses promised the people that God would go before them and fight their battles. Are you trying to run ahead of God?

label: March 29
what_now_1: The man that was possessed by a legion of demons was sent out by Christ as a missionary, Jesus told him to go and share his story. How are you sharing your story with the people you meet?
what_now_2: Moses reminded Israel that their children had not personally experienced the miracles of the Exodus, it was the responsibility of the parents to share the rich history of God's activity. How are you sharing what God is doing in your life with your children and others?

label: March 30
what_now_1: The stories of Jairus and the woman with the issue of blood reveal the faith and desperation of those who approached Jesus. Are you desperate in your approach to Christ... or complacent?
what_now_2: The temptation to worship other gods still exists; What do you do to resist temptation?

label: March 31
what_now_1: The ruler's of Israel were instructed to study the law on a daily basis in order to faithfully obey the Lord's commands. Few kings heeded this command and the results were disastrous for Israel. Pray that your earthly leaders would heed the wisdom of God in their leadership.
what_now_2: Who do you say that Jesus is? Does your life support your assertion? If you were placed on trial for being a follower of Christ, what evidence would you provide?

label: April 1
what_now_1: The strength of Israel's army resided in God. Do you trust in God's strength or your own?
what_now_2: Fear can be contagious like a virus, but God can strengthen and protect us from its spread. Do not allow the fear of those who are afraid infect you. #fearnot

label: April 2
what_now_1: Deuteronomy mixes instructions on safety, purity and social graces. God cares for His people in every area of their lives. No part of your life is separated from God's influence. Are there parts of your life you tend to compartmentalize and separate from God's influence?
what_now_2: James and John, appropriately nicknamed the Sons of Thunder, wanted to call down fire on those that rejected Jesus. The rebuke of Jesus reveals that rejecting Jesus is punishment enough.

label: April 3
what_now_1: God commands Israel to look after the 'outcasts' of society. He makes accommodations for the poor, the widows, the aliens and the hired workers. What things cause you to ignore the 'least of these?'
what_now_2: Psalm 75:9 (NLT): But as for me, I will always proclaim what God has done; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

label: April 4
what_now_1: The story of the Exodus is often repeated in the Old Testament. While it might seem repetitive to us, we must remember that this miraculous event was their story of God's intervention and deliverance. Similarly, we must never tire of the story of Christ's crucifixion and resurrection!
what_now_2: People often justify their actions by saying that they are just a 'Martha'. Remember that Jesus rebuked Martha! Meditate on how you can practice an attitude like Mary.

label: April 5
what_now_1: The writer of Proverbs introduces a new concept... reckless speaking! When are you guilty of reckless speech that wounds the people around you? Ask God to help you control your tongue.
what_now_2: When the Holy Spirit removes sinful habits from our lives, we must strive to replace those habits with positive fruit (the Fruit of the Spirit), because if we simply remove the bad, we will likely only fill the void with other sinful habits. What fruit is God seeking to cultivate in your life?

label: April 6
what_now_1: Luke 12:4-5a (NLT): Dear friends, don't be afraid of those who want to kill your body; they cannot do any more to you after that. But I'll tell you whom to fear. Fear God... | Biblical Truth: Those who have a holy fear of God need not fear any man.
what_now_2: The clothes of the Israelites did not wear during their 40 years in the wilderness. -- 'But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.' ~ Luke 12:7 (KJV) -- It is encouraging to know that God cares for the smallest details of our lives, even when we are in exile! Make a list of the small details that God has taken care of in your life this week.

label: April 7
what_now_1: God prohibited Moses from leading Israel into the land of Promise. Perhaps the people needed to recognize that God, not Moses, had provided the Promised Land. Are you tempted to trust in human leaders more than in God?
what_now_2: Jesus reminds us that if we are ashamed of Him on earth, He will be ashamed of us in heaven. Ask God to grant you the courage to share your faith with confidence.

label: April 8
what_now_1: The Proverb reflects the reality of the familiar axiom, 'Idle hands are the devil's workshop.' When does lack of activity tempt you to sin?
what_now_2: Although Jesus is the Prince of peace, His peaceful reign will only come after conflict. Though we must treat people with love and respect, we must not compromise Truth for peace in our conversations. Are there times when you are tempted to tell people what they want to hear instead of what they need to hear?

label: April 9
what_now_1: Jesus is a fanatic for fruit. He desires, expects and longs for fruit in His followers. Fruit (a changed life) is not optional. How are you bearing fruit? What adjustments can you make to improve your fruit production?
what_now_2: Anxiety in a man's heart weighs him down, but a good word makes him glad. ~ Proverbs 12;25 (ESV) | Biblical Truth: The prescription for anxiety and fear is a good word from God, obtained through prayer and put to use in faith. ~ Source: Fearful To Fearless "

label: April 10
what_now_1: This time the report from the spies was one of courage and confidence. We must face the obstacles that God places in our path with confidence in His power! What obstacle is God empowering you to overcome?
what_now_2: Joshua had some pretty big shoes to fill! The reminder to be strong and courageous must have helped the new leader. Is God calling you out of your comfort zone? Perhaps you need to apply these words today as well. -- Joshua 1:9 (NLT): This is my command-be strong and courageous! Do not be afraid or discouraged. For the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.

label: April 11
what_now_1: God instructed Israel to build a monument to remind them of their entrance into the land of Promise. It is important to mark milestones of your journey with God. Take a moment to reflect on the monumental moments of your faith journey.
what_now_2: God wanted the people to know that He was still in control in spite of the death of Moses. Are there situations you face where God is trying to remind you of His control?

label: April 12
what_now_1: Are you harboring sin like Achan? Ask the Holy Spirit to reveal areas in your life where you need to seek God's forgiveness and reveal the clean slate that He offers.
what_now_2: The Lord is not on our side, we must align ourselves with Him to make sure we are on His side. How does your life reflect that you are on the Lord's side?

label: April 13
what_now_1: Do you need to place a sentry over your lips? Ask God to guard your tongue!
what_now_2: Then the LORD said to Joshua, 'Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged. Take the whole army with you, and go up and attack Ai. For I have delivered into your hands the king of Ai, his people, his city and his land.' ~ Joshua 8:1 (NIV) -- Faithful repentance leads to expectant comfort from God who is able to deliver us from fear and discouragement.

label: April 14
what_now_1: The parable of Lazarus and the rich man reminds us that we should live for eternity rather than temporary fulfillment.
what_now_2: Don't ever be afraid or discouraged, Joshua told his men. 'Be strong and courageous, for the LORD is going to do this to all of your enemies.' ~ Joshua 10:25 (NLT) -- We have victory over fear through Christ who has already defeated the enemy.

label: April 15
what_now_1: With my whole being, body and soul, I will shout joyfully to the living God. - Psalm 84:2b (NLT)
what_now_2: 'The kingdom of God is within you.' (Luke 17:21) ~ How is the Kingdom of God being lived out in your life today?

label: April 16
what_now_1: Joshua has only recently come to lead Israel, but he has been active for a long time as an assistant to Moses. It changes our perspective to consider that Joshua led the attack on the Promised Land at a ripe old age. What type of Kingdom Work have you convinced yourself that you are too old to accomplish? Remember, we never retire from obedience to God!
what_now_2: Psalm 85:8a (NLT): I listen carefully to what God the Lord is saying, for he speaks peace to his faithful people.

label: April 17
what_now_1: Pride leads to conflict, wisdom leads to collaboration. Do you often find yourself in conflict with others? How might you temper your pride?
what_now_2: The story of the rich ruler emphasizes our dependency on God for salvation. We cannot earn it!

label: April 18
what_now_1: The parable of the minas indicates that we must be willing to trust God enough to take risks. The third servant is rebuked for maintaining the status quo and failing to increase the investment he received. How is God calling you to invest what He has given you?
what_now_2: The story of Zacchaeus is often relegated to the children's department, but it reminds adults of the grace of God and the extravagant response of the forgiven sinner.

label: April 19
what_now_1: The provision for cities of refuge reveals God's desire to protect His people by providing a means of mercy and justice for those who accidentally took a life. How are you extending God's mercy and justice?
what_now_2: 'If they keep quiet, the stones will cry out.' (Luke 19:40) ~ Are the rocks around you crying out to cover your lack of heart-felt worship? Spend time praising God with a favorite hymn or chorus, or better yet, write your own song of praise!

label: April 20
what_now_1: When the Reubenites, Gadites and half-tribe of Manasseh built a questionable altar, the rest of Israel rushed to the defense of righteousness. How do you react when you see 'Christians' engaged in questionable behavior? Ask God for courage to confront and rebuke if necessary.
what_now_2: Psalm 89:1a NLT: I will sing of the Lord's unfailing love forever!

label: April 21
what_now_1: Make a list of the things that you do to impress the people around you... now make another list of the things you do to impress God.
what_now_2: The Sadducees did not believe in the resurrection and were trying to trick Jesus. Do you allow 'deep theological questions' to derail your faith and obedience? Remember that His ways are not our ways, and that the primary purpose of God's Word is life transformation!

label: April 22
what_now_1: Joshua and Moses both pleaded with the people to honor God with their lives. Are you honoring God with your life?
what_now_2: What are you doing to make sure that your household serves the Lord?

label: April 23
what_now_1: Like the warnings of Joshua and Moses in the Old Testament, Jesus offers a warning against dissipation, drunkeness and the anxieties of life. What is weighing on your heart and preventing you from walking with Christ?
what_now_2: Caleb provides an example of boldness as he drives out giants and provides for his family. How can you imitate Caleb's boldness today?

label: April 24
what_now_1: Even in periods of rebellion, God reveals His mercy on Israel by sending judges to win victories and draw Israel back to their God. Thank God for the mercy He has shown you in the midst of rebellion.
what_now_2: Peter was ready to follow Jesus to death. But was Peter ready to follow Jesus in life? Why is it sometimes more difficult to live for Christ than to die for Him?

label: April 25
what_now_1: The song of Deborah seems to praise righteous leaders who are willing to live for the Kingdom of God. How can you pray for your spiritual leaders today?
what_now_2: Like Peter, we are willing to fight for Jesus. While there are times to fight, there are also times to lay down our swords. Pray that God would help you differentiate between the two.

label: April 26
what_now_1: God saw Gideon as a mighty warrior although Gideon's present circumstances screamed the opposite. What does God see in you? If God is calling you to a task that seems out of character, remember God knows you better than you know yourself.
what_now_2: Peter's denial of Jesus challenges us because we often deny our Savior by our words and actions. Have you betrayed God with your words or actions this week? Ask God to forgive you of your betrayal.

label: April 27
what_now_1: '...Father, forgive them, for they don't know what they are doing.' (Luke 23:34 NLT) ~ As a follower of Jesus, how can you practice this type of forgiveness?
what_now_2: The thief on the cross tells us its never too late to find Jesus! Have you given up on someone? Pray that God reveal Himself.

label: April 28
what_now_1: The disciples initially doubted the resurrection report of the women. How does your life reflect your belief in the resurrection?
what_now_2: 'Into your hands I commit my Spirit.' (Luke 23:46) ~ Jesus completed his mission to seek and save the lost. Thank God that your salvation is secured in the completed work of Christ!

label: April 29
what_now_1: Jesus tells the disciples that they are His witnesses. What sort of witness for the resurrected Jesus have you been this week?
what_now_2: We often navigate life with our own internal GPS. The Proverb warns that our GPS might seem right to us but unless it is calibrated with the Holy Spirit, we will likely veer far off God's course! Ask the Holy Spirit to calibrate your GPS!

label: April 30
what_now_1: Jesus existed with God before the beginning of time. There has never been a day He didn't exist, nor will there be a day he doesn't exist.
what_now_2: Jephthah overcame the stigma of being the son of a prostitute to lead Israel. Remember that God is more concerned with where you are headed than with where you have been. Are there issues from your past that you allow to hinder your present progress in Christ?

label: May 1
what_now_1: Without the witness of Andrew, Peter would never have discovered Jesus... Is there a Peter waiting to hear about Jesus from you?
what_now_2: Manoah is not the first parent to ask God for wisdom on raising their child. Parents, ask God for wisdom today. Remember, even adult children need the influence of a Godly parent.

label: May 2
what_now_1: God casts our sin as far as the east is from the west. What confessed sin is the accuser bringing back to your mind today? Remember, confessed sin that is brought back to our mind, is brought by Satan not God.
what_now_2: While Samson destroys many Philistines with his final act, we can only imagine what he could have done without his flaws. What flaws are hindering your effectiveness in the Kingdom of God?

label: May 3
what_now_1: Micah's mother helped direct him toward idolatry. Parents, are you encouraging your children to follow idols? How can you steer them away from idols?
what_now_2: 'For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.' ~ Perhaps you can quote John 3:16... more importantly, are you living John 3:16? How can the world see the truth of John 3:16 in your life and in your actions?

label: May 4
what_now_1: The tribes of Israel react to the sin of Gibeah. How do you hold fellow believers accountable for their actions?
what_now_2: For the second time in Judges we are told that Israel had no king. The Lord was supposed to be their king, but they refused to honor Him. Does your life reflect that God is your king?

label: May 5
what_now_1: The woman at the well received grace from Jesus. Who do you know that is in need of God's grace today?
what_now_2: '...everyone did as he saw fit.' (Judges 21:25) - this line from Judges is descriptive of our culture. What must you do to break the mold and submit yourself and your family to God?

label: May 6
what_now_1: The man with the sick son 'took Jesus at His word.' (John 4:50) Are there times you have trouble taking Jesus at His Word? Ask God to help you trust Him.
what_now_2: When we turn our concerns over to God we are assured that He will work it for His good. #fearnot #365fearnots

label: May 7
what_now_1: Hannah battled for her husband's affections with another woman. Men, does your wife battle for your affections with your hobby, career, or other form of 'mistress'? Commit to give your affections fully to your spouse.
what_now_2: If Jesus could accomplish nothing apart from the Father, don't you think you need to remain connected to Him as well? Take a look at your calendar. Do you need to make changes to your calendar in order to remain more connected to the Father?

label: May 8
what_now_1: 'Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his love endures forever.' ~ Psalm 106:1. | Thank God for His enduring love!
what_now_2: Pray the prayer of Samuel today; 'Speak, for your servant is listening.' ~ 1 Samuel 3:10

label: May 9
what_now_1: No idols stand in the presence of God! What idols are interfering with your worship?
what_now_2: Ebenezer means stone of help. Think back to the instances in your life where God has helped you. Give thanks for His past activity, His promises for the future, and let them remind you of His continued presence!

label: May 10
what_now_1: Peer pressure led the nation of Israel to beg for a king. If we are not careful, God will fulfill our requests. Are you asking God for something that contradicts His plan?
what_now_2: Do you seek miraculous signs as evidence of God's presence? While God works miracles, His activity in the simple things of life is more frequent. Thank God for working in the mundane aspects of your life.

label: May 11
what_now_1: Offer gentle answers to everyone you meet today.
what_now_2: God selected Saul to lead Israel. As we will see in the days to come, Saul is a flawed leader. Yet, God made him king over Israel. We must remember the instructions of the New Testament that we should pray for our political leaders, whether we agree with them politically or not.

label: May 12
what_now_1: Saul's impatience proved to be his downfall. Is impatience hindering your spiritual growth?
what_now_2: Samuel said that he would not commit the sin of failing to pray for the nation of Israel. (1 Samuel 12:23) - How can you pray for your country?

label: May 13
what_now_1: Jonathan shows great courage and confidence in God when he attacks the Philistines. How is God calling you to show courage today?

label: May 14
what_now_1: Partial obedience is disobedience! God desires obedience over sacrifice and ritual. Are there areas of your life you are living in partial obedience?
what_now_2: Samuel tried to select a king for Israel based on external indicators. How often do we make the same mistake when we interact with others? Ask God to help you see others with His eyes.

label: May 15
what_now_1: David wasn't motivated by reward or prestige, he wanted to honor God and defend His glory. How will you defend God's glory today?
what_now_2: What giants are taunting you today? God still empowers slingshots!

label: May 16
what_now_1: Jonathan played the difficult role of mediator. At times we must work to bring about resolution in the hearts of the people around us. Do you need to serve as a mediator today?
what_now_2: Jesus said to the people who believed in him, 'You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teachings. And you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.' ~ John 8:31-32 (NLT)

label: May 17
what_now_1: Jonathan risked serious repercussions and displayed great integrity by supporting David. How are you showing that same courage and integrity in support of your friends?
what_now_2: Do you praise God from the rising of the sun until it sets? Make a point to pause in the midst of your day today to praise God for His abundant blessings.

label: May 18
what_now_1: While David fled the wrath of Saul, individuals displayed kindness and concern for David's cause. Has someone come to your assistance in recent weeks? Thank God for them and then let them know that their actions are appreciated.
what_now_2: David sought the Lord's wisdom. Are you seeking God's wisdom or have you ventured out on your own wisdom?

label: May 19
what_now_1: '...no one can snatch them out of my hand. (John 10:28) -- This statement by Jesus is one of the clearest revelations of the eternal security of those who believe in Christ. Praise God that your salvation is centered on Christ's strength and not your own!
what_now_2: Abigail brokered peace between David and Nabal. She prohibited the otherwise inevitable conflict. How is God calling you to be a peacemaker today?

label: May 20
what_now_1: When the Lord did not answer Saul he turned to witchcraft. Lack of patience will lead us to listen to voices other than God's. When are you tempted to listen to a voice that isn't God's?
what_now_2: Jesus lingers before going to visit His friend Lazarus. While the delay might be considered indifference, it provides a forum for Jesus to work an incredible miracle. When have apparent delays and detours resulted in God's miraculous activity in your life?

label: May 21
what_now_1: While David was living with his enemies (the Philistines), a raiding party captured his family and the familes of his men. In the midst of potential mutiny, David found the strength of God and recovered the captured family members. David's situation provides an example for us in difficult times. How do you turn to God on dark days?
what_now_2: 'It is better to take refuge in the Lord than to trust in people.' ~ Psalm 118:8 (NLT)

label: May 22
what_now_1: When it comes to investing your resources, are you tempted to invest in the enhancement of your earthly life? How can you better invest in eternity?
what_now_2: Even after Saul's death, David continues to seek the Lord's direction. In what areas are you tempted to be overconfident? Continue to seek God's face!

label: May 23
what_now_1: Joab did not display honor or obedience in the murder of Abner. How is bitterness hindering your ability to honor God's commands?
what_now_2: Do you hide God's word in your heart? Why not begin by memorizing; 'I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.' ~ Psalm 119:11 (NLT)

label: May 24
what_now_1: Even after David became king and experienced success he continued to inquire of the Lord. (2 Samuel 5:23) In what area do you need to seek God's input today? "
what_now_2: When the journey of life grows wearisome, remember that God is preparing an eternal abode that will eclipse anything on this earth!

label: May 25
what_now_1: Sometimes a no from God reveals a hidden yes! David would not build God's Temple, but one of his descendants would. God reveals that David would always have a son on the throne of Israel. Is God providing a hidden yes in what seems like an apparent no in your life?
what_now_2: 'Turn my eyes from worthless things, and give me life through your word.' ~ Psalm 119:37 (NLT) -- What 'worthless things' are competing for your attention?

label: May 26
what_now_1: David wasn't in the right place (at war) when he succumbed to temptation with Bathsheba. Are you opening the door to sin by placing yourself in the 'wrong place'?
what_now_2: David compounds his sin by attempting to cover up the affair. Are you lying to cover up your sin? Come clean today and receive the cleansing available through confession.

label: May 27
what_now_1: Confronted with his sin; David confessed and sought God's forgiveness. Do you need to seek God's grace today?
what_now_2: God used Nathan to reveal David's sin to the king. God has given us the Holy Spirit. Don't overlook the inner voice of conviction that draws you back to God. What is the Holy Spirit speaking to you about?

label: May 28
what_now_1: David handles the rape of Tamar poorly. Though he is angry, he never punishes his son, Amnon. This leads to the rage and eventual rebellion of Absalom. In parenting and in life, we cannot ignore sin and conflict. Ignored sin and conflict will fester and infect. Is there conflict you hope will go away?
what_now_2: Be encouraged that Jesus prayed for your protection in the world. -- 'Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name that you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.' ~ John 17:11 (CEV)

label: May 29
what_now_1: Joab modeled peacemaking when he intervened between David and Absalom even though it did not result in restoration. How can you be a peacemaker in your home, on your job, or in the church?
what_now_2: As David flees Jerusalem, he finds some who are loyal and some who turn on him. Can your friends count on your loyalty in times of desperate need?

label: May 30
what_now_1: As Jesus revealed, Pilate is ultimately an instrument of God's grand design. We must remember this as we consider our contemporary political climate. Rulers are ordained by God. Many times we cannot understand why certain leaders achieve power, but we submit ourselves to God's authority as we submit ourselves to their authority! Pray for your earthly leaders today.
what_now_2: David displays great faith and restraint as he departs the city. He entrusts his fate to God. What circumstances in your life currently demand that type of trust on your part?

label: May 31
what_now_1: Many people supported David during Absalom's revolt. In desperate times it is important to realize that we are not alone. God has provided a church family and friends to walk with us! Make a list of the people you know that will support you on the darkest days, then be sure to thank them for their friendship.
what_now_2: What irony that Jesus, the Living Water, would cry out, 'I am thirsty.' (John 19:28) There are people around you every day who are thirsting for Living Water. How can you make a delivery?

label: June 1
what_now_1: The miraculous is always hard to believe, that is the nature of miracles! The resurrection is no different. In spite of Jesus' predictions, the empty tomb baffles the disciples. Do you expect God to work miracles in your life? Expect the unexpected in your walk with Christ!
what_now_2: The book of John was recorded to convince people that Jesus is the Christ. How can your life serve the same purpose?

label: June 2
what_now_1: Shimei deserved to die. He had disrespected the king and now the king was returning victorious. Yet David will not allow his warrior to end Shimei's life. Extending grace requires much more strength that exacting revenge. How can you imitate David's grace?
what_now_2: Joab jealously eliminates a potential rival in Amasa. Are there individuals that arouse jealousy within you? If so, ask God to help you overcome this jealousy.

label: June 3
what_now_1: It is not clear whether Mephibosheth side with Absalom or not, but David again spares his life. In what ways does David's treatment of Mephibosheth remind you of God's grace?
what_now_2: Sin always has consequences. The sin of Saul caused Israel to suffer through a famine. Where do you see consequences from sin in your life, in the life of your church, and in the life of your country?

label: June 4
what_now_1: David's mighty men were renewed in their strength and valor. God needs mighty men and women to carry out His mission today. How can you stand courageously for God?
what_now_2: Peter proclaimed the first truly Christian sermon at Pentecost. Read Peter's words carefully; can you boil down the core message of the Gospel in three or four sentences?

label: June 5
what_now_1: Concentrate on making your words pleasant today so that you might encourage and instruct those around you.
what_now_2: Who do you know that is longing for times of refreshment? (Acts 3:19) Ask God to give you a heart for those who are suffering from spiritual thirst.

label: June 6
what_now_1: The message of salvation (Acts 4:12) runs contrary to the cultural agenda, but it is the foundational truth of God's Word. Redemption comes through Christ alone!
what_now_2: David did not interfere with Adonijah and correct his misbehavior. Parents, do not assume that misbehaving children will correct themselves. That is your job!

label: June 7
what_now_1: As David prepares for death he gives Solomon a blessing and a challenge. Dads how are you blessing and challenging your sons to walk in the Lord's ways?
what_now_2: After the disciples are arrested they show great boldness by obeying God and returning to the city to proclaim the Good News. Ask God to give you courage to share the gospel and for the ability to find joy in times of persecution.

label: June 8
what_now_1: Solomon was given great wealth, wisdom and honor. What would you do with those gifts? What are you doing with the talents, treasures and abilities that God has entrusted to you?
what_now_2: Psalm 126 describes the joy of Israel as they returned to the land of promise. Take a few moments today to rejoice in the eternal home that God has promised you.

label: June 9
what_now_1: What Temple-sized task has God commissioned you to complete? What Temple-sized tasks can you help others complete?
what_now_2: 'Psalm 127:1 (NLT): Unless the Lord builds a house, the work of the builders is wasted...' | What are you trying to accomplish using your own strength?

label: June 10
what_now_1: Solomon utilized individuals skilled in craftsmanship to construct the Temple. Are you missing out on opportunities to utilize your own talents for the glory of God!
what_now_2: We are given a hint that something is amiss in Solomon's life. He invested more time on his palace (13 years) than he did on the Temple (11 years). Are you investing more time and energy in your personal interests than in God's Kingdom plans?

label: June 11
what_now_1: Stephen calls his accusers 'stiff-necked.' It took incredible boldness for him to call out his accusers. How can you display that type of boldness in your conversations?
what_now_2: Do you spend so much of your time earning money that you miss out on enjoying life? Write down three things you can do today to enjoy life... then do them!

label: June 12
what_now_1: If you, Lord, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand? But with you there is forgiveness, so that we can, with reverence, serve you. ~ Psalm 130:3-4 (NIV)
what_now_2: The Ethiopian could not understand the gospel without Philip's help. What can you do to make the Good News more clear to those who don't understand it?

label: June 13
what_now_1: Solomon's marriage led him to worship despicable gods. (Molech and Chemosh required child sacrifice!) A small crack in the believer's life can quickly become a giant chasm! Where are you allowing cracks to develop in your spiritual life?
what_now_2: 'Saul spent several days with the disciples in Damascus.' (Acts 9:19) While we often think of Paul's conversion as a brief event, we discover that he was discipled over time by other followers of Christ. Is someone discipling you? Who are you discipling?

label: June 14
what_now_1: The man of God spoke boldly against the idolatry of Jeroboam. How is God calling you to speak boldly about idolatry in the lives of those around you?
what_now_2: What does Acts convey to you as you read that the church was 'living in the fear of the Lord.' (Acts 9:31). Does that have any implications for you? For your church?

label: June 15
what_now_1: Nothing is hidden from the sight of God. Do you need to spend some time today dealing with things you have pretended He could not see.
what_now_2: Asa risked strained family relationships to stand up for righteousness. How willing are you to take risks for your faith?

label: June 16
what_now_1: The message God revealed to Peter through Cornelius reminds us that the whole world needs the gospel. How are you personally contributing to reaching that goal?
what_now_2: Jeroboam eventually received punishment for his sin. Have you been frustrated that some seem to get away with sinning? Remember, sin always carries consequences.

label: June 17
what_now_1: Do you find yourself wavering between the opinion of God and the opinion of the world? What can you do to get off the fence?
what_now_2: Obadiah faced a crisis of belief. He had already risked his life and now Elijah asked him to take another step of faith. How is God challenging you to continue the journey of faith?

label: June 18
what_now_1: The greatest attacks often come on the heels of our greatest victories. If you have experienced recent successes, ask God to protect you!
what_now_2: The church was praying for Peter's release, yet they were shocked by Peter's presence. When has God surprised you with answered prayers?

label: June 19
what_now_1: In spite of Ahab's wealth, he wanted what was not his. This desire led to murder. Are you guilty of coveting?
what_now_2: Seek wisdom over wealth!

label: June 20
what_now_1: Paul traces the redemption of God, revealed fully in Christ, through the history of Israel. As you read the Old Testament, remember that all of the events led to the cross.
what_now_2: Through Christ we are justified of our sin. Spend time thanking God for this unmerited gift of grace.

label: June 21
what_now_1: People talk of going out in a 'blaze of glory.' Elijah's exceptional exit certainly fits that description. While your exit might not be as dramatic, your arrival will be just as spectacular. Meditate for a few moments on what it will be like to arrive in the presence of God.
what_now_2: Paul and Barnabas offered Truth to the people of Pisidian Antioch. Unfortunately, some of the religious leaders were jealous and they defended the status quo by stirring up dissent. When are you tempted to reject Truth in order to defend the status quo?

label: June 22
what_now_1: Paul picked himself up after stones pummeled his body, he returned to proclaim the gospel to the very people who had hurled the rocks. What does this teach you about holding a grudge?
what_now_2: God provided oil for the woman facing desperate poverty. The only limit of God's gift was the amount of jars the woman collected. Are you limiting God's ability to work through you by your lack of faith?

label: June 23
what_now_1: Naaman almost missed the Lord's healing because the 'solution' was too simple. When have you been guilty of overcomplicating God's commands?
what_now_2: Make Psalm 141:3-4 your prayer today: 'Help me to guard my words whenever I say something. Don't let me want to do evil or waste my time doing wrong with wicked people. Don't let me even taste the good things they offer.' (CEV)

label: June 24
what_now_1: If you doubt the Lord's ability, you will rarely witness His power. Ask God to help you expect amazing things from Him.
what_now_2: Then Elisha prayed, 'O LORD, open his eyes and let him see!' ~ Ask God to open your eyes to see things as they really are. God is larger than anything. Don't make things out to be worse than they are.

label: June 25
what_now_1: The kings of Israel and Judah refuse to obey and honor God. Time and again they choose the path of least resistance rather than following God. As you look at your life, do you typically obey or are you like the leaders of Israel and Judah and follow your own path? Ask God to strengthen you, that you might follow His path!
what_now_2: Beaten and bloodied, chained to each other in the bellows of a Roman prison, Paul and Silas praised God. What an unlikely location for worship. When have you praised God in the trials?

label: June 26
what_now_1: Not everyone who writes a book or preaches a sermon has a true word from the Lord! The Bereans received Paul's message with great eagerness and tested it against Scripture. How do you examine the messages you hear or read?
what_now_2: You reap what you sow! In the gruesome account from 2 Kings many evil leaders, like Jezebel, receive their punishment. Don't pretend that your sin will not find you.

label: June 27
what_now_1: If you are attempting to make a break from a heritage of unrighteousness, take comfort in the example of Joash who broke with family tradition and sought to honor God. Ask God to give you the strength and courage of Joash.
what_now_2: One night the Lord spoke to Paul in a vision and told him, Don't be afraid! Speak out! Don't be silent! For I am with you, and no one will attack and harm you, for many people in this city belong to me. ~ Acts 18:9-10 (NLT)

label: June 28
what_now_1: God allowed the nation of Israel to experience oppression because of the wickedness of her kings. If you find yourself living under the yoke of oppression, ask God if you are living in sin. (Your struggles are not necessarily due to your sin, but if they are, wouldn't you like to know?)
what_now_2: Do you enjoy hearing yourself talk? Ask God to help you spend more time listening and less time talking.

label: June 29
what_now_1: Ahaz sacrificed his son to the false gods of the land. Are you offering your time, family, money... to a false god? Remember, the wrong god always demands the wrong sacrifice!
what_now_2: The sons of Sceva attempted to do the things of God without the power of God and received a beating for it. What are you trying to do on your own strength?

label: June 30
what_now_1: Paul balanced rebuke with encouragement. How are you at striking that balance?
what_now_2: Sins committed in secret never escape the All-Seeing eyes of God. What sins do you think you are hiding from Him?

label: July 1
what_now_1: Like Goliath, Sennacherib taunted Israel and Israel's God. Who or what taunts you and your God today? Remember that God is greater than any obstacle that you face.
what_now_2: In utter desperation Hezekiah cried out to God. Don't wait until the situation is dire to involve Almighty God! Cry out to God with your struggles today.

label: July 2
what_now_1: Arrogance always catches up with us. Hezekiah showed off his possessions because he forgot that they belonged to the Lord. Which possessions threaten to possess you?
what_now_2: Like Hezekiah, don't give up! Keep praying!

label: July 3
what_now_1: The people of Israel had literally lost the commands of God. Josiah drew the attention of the people to God's written Word. As you are reading the Word of God this year, draw the attention of others to this life-giving word!
what_now_2: The Word of God brings revival to the people of God. Allow the Word to bring revival to your heart, that you might infect others with the power of God!

label: July 4
what_now_1: Paul reveals the hypocrisy of the religious leaders. Legalists tend to emphasize the laws that they keep best or the ones that best suit their interests. Ask God to help you avoid the trap of legalism.
what_now_2: Make a concerted effort to listen before you speak today.

label: July 5
what_now_1: The Roman officials work to keep Paul safe from the Jews. God utilizes those in authority even when we might not admire or appreciate them. How can you show respect to the authorities in your life?
what_now_2: Today's reminder from God's message to Paul is simple, 'Take courage!' (Acts 23:11) ...in God's strength.

label: July 6
what_now_1: Paul spoke of righteousness and self-control before a leader who had stolen the wife of his brother. How can you stand up for righteousness?
what_now_2: Paul is on trial for His faith. If placed on trial for your faith, what evidence would be utilized to prove that you believe in Christ?

label: July 7
what_now_1: Paul's determined defense attracted King Agrippa. Are people drawn to you by your thoughtful description of your faith?
what_now_2: Lay your requests before God today expecting a response from Him.

label: July 8
what_now_1: The Reubenites, Gadites, and half tribe of Manasseh experienced victory because they trusted God. Do you believe that God is able to overcome the obstacles in your life?
what_now_2: Proverbs 18:20-21 (GNT): 'You will have to live with the consequences of everything you say. What you say can preserve life or destroy it; so you must accept the consequences of your words.' ... Choose your words wisely!

label: July 9
what_now_1: The genealogical record reminds us that the people of God are not immune to adversity. The presence of difficulty is not always a sign of God's absence. How might the difficulties in your life be evidence of God's presence?
what_now_2: Paul's experience at sea reinforces the sentiment of the Old Testament reading. Paul courageously obeyed God's instructions only to find himself as a prisoner in a storm at sea.

label: July 10
what_now_1: Saul's sad epitaph points out his failure to inquire of the Lord. While David constantly sought God's direction, Saul refused to seek God's wisdom. Don't make Saul's mistake! Are you seeking God's wisdom today?
what_now_2: Paul shows concern for his captors in the shipwreck. How can you show kindness to your enemies?

label: July 11
what_now_1: While it is easy to attribute David's rise to power to his sharp leadership, his military power or his shrewd political maneuvers, the Bible is clear that David prospered because the Lord was with him. What evidence in your life reveals that the Lord is with you?
what_now_2: Paul's imprisonment enabled him to share the gospel unhindered in Rome. From this political and cultural center, God used Paul to advance the Kingdom. Remember that the adversity in your life might be a part of God's plan to fulfill His purpose!

label: July 12
what_now_1: Think about your daily conversations and habits and how they reflect on the gospel of Christ. What can you do to help people see or hear the gospel in you?
what_now_2: The anger of the Lord directed at Uzzah offers a fierce reminder of the Lord's holiness. Are there any areas in which you take the holiness of God for granted?

label: July 13
what_now_1: David led Israel in celebrating the placement of the ark in Jerusalem. Sometimes our busy schedules result in our missing out in celebrating God's victorious provision. What blessings do you need to celebrate today?
what_now_2: Romans 1:20 (NLT): For ever since the world was created, people have seen the earth and sky. Through everything God made, they can clearly see his invisible qualities-his eternal power and divine nature. So they have no excuse for not knowing God.

label: July 14
what_now_1: Do you have a tendency to look down on those around you, casting judgment on their sins while ignoring your own? Ask God to replace your judgmental spirit with a merciful spirit.
