what_now_2: David wanted to build a Temple for God, but God said no. Are you trying to 'do something' that God has instructed you to leave alone? We can never honor God while disobeying his instructions.

label: July 15
what_now_1: If we think grace gives us the freedom to sin, we have completely missed the purpose of grace and the cost of Christ's sacrifice. Meditate on Christ's sacrifice and how that impacts your view of sin.
what_now_2: Remember that sometimes even forgiven sin bears physical consequences. Thank God for forgiveness and ask for His strength to endure the consequences.

label: July 16
what_now_1: The next time that you are repulsed by a public sin, remind yourself that your sins are just as grotesque in the eyes of God.
what_now_2: Only, may the LORD grant you discretion and understanding, that when he gives you charge over Israel you may keep the law of the LORD your God. Then you will prosper if you are careful to observe the statutes and the rules that the LORD commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and courageous. Fear not; do not be dismayed. -- 1 Chronicles 22:12-13 (ESV)

label: July 17
what_now_1: God credited Abraham with righteousness based on his faith. He did not earn righteousness by obedience, his obedience sprang out of faith. Are you trying to manufacture obedience without faith? It will never work!
what_now_2: Do you listen to the instructions of others? Too many times we barrel through life like a two-year old, unwilling to heed the warnings of those who have already experienced life. Seek out an elder who can speak wisdom into your life today.

label: July 18
what_now_1: God promised Abraham that his descendants would be more numerous than the sand on the seashore. Abraham didn't live to see the promise fulfilled, though the record in Chronicles reveals the impressive development of the nation of Israel. God keeps His promises. What promises are you believing God might fulfill after your lifetime?
what_now_2: Paul reveals the hypocrisy of the religious leaders. Legalists tend to emphasize that the laws they keep best are the ones that best suit their interests. Ask God for help in avoiding the trap of legalism.

label: July 19
what_now_1: David knew that the Temple would be an arduous task for Solomon. He warned against fear and discouragement. Ask God to give you courage to face the task overwhelming you today.
what_now_2: David offers direct instructions to Solomon, complete with a warning against disobedience. Parents, how are you providing your children direct guidelines for Godly living?

label: July 20
what_now_1: Solomon passed up earthly wealth to request earthly wisdom. Ask God to grant you the wisdom to accomplish His purpose in your life.
what_now_2: The psalmist reminds that God will not leave us. (Psalm 16:10) As you go through your day, remember that God goes with you.

label: July 21
what_now_1: Have you ever felt that everyone was against you? David obviously struggled with these issues, too. Follow the great king's lead and recognize that God will not forsake you.
what_now_2: The Hebrew people knew how to celebrate God's victories. What should you celebrate today? Invite some friends over and celebrate God's blessings.

label: July 22
what_now_1: God's presence interrupted Solomon's worship service. Pray that God will interrupt your worship with the power of His presence. Ask God to stir within His church and ignite a revival among His people.
what_now_2: 2 Chronicles 7:14 (KJV): If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.

label: July 23
what_now_1: Rehoboam's failure reminds us of the value of wise counsel. Do you tend to surround yourself with people who tell you what you want to hear or do you seek the wisdom of those who disagree?
what_now_2: If you accept your sinfulness as human nature, you deny the activity of the Spirit in you. Remember, the power of the resurrection can transform your life and overcome your sinful nature. Ask God to empower you in your battle against sin.

label: July 24
what_now_1: The Lord allowed his people to endure chastisement in order to draw them to Himself. God will do the same in your life. Is God trying to reveal something to you about disobedience? Don't ignore his discipline!
what_now_2: Sometimes our most painful prayers are inaudible to human ears. At those times the Spirit intercedes and interprets for us. Don't be afraid to pray in groans and tears.

label: July 25
what_now_1: Asa had the courage to stop the tide of spiritual rebellion in Judah. Are you willing to stand against that same tide today?
what_now_2: The courage and commitment of Asa's younger years wanes as the king ages. He begins to trust in diplomacy over God's activity. How do you sense your fervor for God fading? Ask God to restore the passion in your life and avoid the arrogance of Asa.

label: July 26
what_now_1: Jehoshaphat destroyed the high places, which were a stronghold of spiritual darkness. What strongholds linger in your life? What habits, attitudes, and actions divert you from God?
what_now_2: David speaks of those who trusted in horses and chariots. (Psalm 20:7) While the temptation to trust in chariots has passed, the temptation to trust technological advancements still exists. When are you tempted to trust technology?

label: July 27
what_now_1: Often the people of God experienced victory without lifting a weapon. Too often we attempt to win the battle in our strength rather than allowing Almighty God to handle the situation. In what situation are you working on your own when you need to be trusting God?
what_now_2: '...how can they hear without someone preaching to them?' (Romans 10:14) Paul's question still reverberates today. Who needs to hear the Good News from you?

label: July 28
what_now_1: Jehoram veered from the righteous path his father, Jehoshaphat, had blazed. How are you honoring your family's legacy of faith? If your family's legacy of faith started with you, how can you leave a strong legacy for your children?
what_now_2: It would have been easy for Jehoiada to go along with the wicked reign of Athaliah. However, he courageously took a stand and led a revolt. Doing the right thing often requires courage and sacrifice. In what ways is God calling you to display courage and sacrifice by standing for Him?

label: July 29
what_now_1: Amaziah worshiped the impotent idols of the Edomites; are there idols in your life?
what_now_2: Don't allow the disease of 'self' erode your soul, ask God to continue transforming you into His image by replacing your self-centered nature with the sacrificial nature of Christ.

label: July 30
what_now_1: Failure to submit to human authority reveals a failure to submit to divine authority. Do you live in submission to the authority figures in your life? Boss? Government officials? Pastor?
what_now_2: The Shepherd Psalm conveys peace and stillness. Allow your Shepherd to remove you from the busyness of the day so that He can provide you with the nourishment you need.

label: July 31
what_now_1: How is God at work in the world around you? Make a concerted effort to look for God's activity today.
what_now_2: Ask God to give you clean hands and a pure heart.

label: August 1
what_now_1: Corporate worship gave the Israelites the courage to smash the sacred stones and cut down the Asherah poles. Do not forsake corporate worship and the courage it can provide! If you do not have a church family, ask God to direct you to a place where you can be a part of His community.
what_now_2: Paul displays a clear sense of calling and purpose. Do you know your purpose? What has God called you to accomplish for His kingdom?

label: August 2
what_now_1: Do you struggle with a proud heart, as Hezekiah did? Repent of your pride and humble yourself before God!
what_now_2: Allow God to be your refuge today. Find the protection and provision that only He can offer.

label: August 3
what_now_1: Unlike most of the kings in Israel, Josiah was faithful from start to finish. Resolve to finish your faith with as much vigor as you started your faith. Ask God to empower you for this journey.
what_now_2: The story of Manasseh's repentance reveals the depths of God's grace. Don't give up on your wayward family members; don't lose heart in your prayers. God can lead even the hardest heart to repentance!

label: August 4
what_now_1: Josiah reinstituted the Passover. Israel had forgotten this feast which was a reminder of God's deliverance. Don't neglect the observance of the Lord's Supper, lest you forget the sacrifice that God provided in Christ.
what_now_2: How do your actions, words and attitudes bring the people of God together?

label: August 5
what_now_1: As was the case in Corinth, the Gospel of Jesus still provokes cynics. It seems too simple, too good to be true. Continue to pray that this simple story of Good News will continue to overwhelm the world!
what_now_2: Cyrus allowed the exiled Jews to return to their homeland. God used the Persian king to set His people free. All kings and rulers are under the authority of God! Pray for your government leaders today.

label: August 6
what_now_1: Think back to the early stages of your walk of faith and how you have grown in Christ? Thank God for those changes as well as those still in progress.
what_now_2: Whenever we seek to do God's work, there will always be opposition. Who is opposing you as you seek to do God's work? Don't mistake opposition for God's absence!

label: August 7
what_now_1: Think back to the reverence that the Jewish people displayed for the Temple. Paul says that you are the Temple of God. What does Paul mean by that statement? How does it impact your life?
what_now_2: God will judge not only our actions but the attitudes that motivated the action. Take a few moments to consider, what motivates your service?

label: August 8
what_now_1: Take a few moments to reflect on the gifts you have been given. How are you doing as a steward of those gifts?
what_now_2: Do you live a life like Paul that encourages people to imitate you? Ask God to help you live in such a way that people will see Christ in you!

label: August 9
what_now_1: Where does sexual immorality threaten to invade your life?
what_now_2: Paul indicates that Christians should be more concerned with sin inside the church than sin outside the church. (1 Corinthians 5:12) What sins need to be addressed within your community of faith?

label: August 10
what_now_1: Grace enables us to live in freedom, but freedom should never be an excuse to abuse grace. Are you guilty of abusing God's grace?
what_now_2: It is not enough to simply confess sin. We must also repent and turn from our sinful actions.

label: August 11
what_now_1: Read Psalm 31 and spend a few moments meditating on the greatness of God's love.
what_now_2: In obedience to God, Nehemiah makes a bold request of the king. What is God calling you to do that will require boldness?

label: August 12
what_now_1: Following God's leadership often invokes opposition. Don't cower to the taunts of men when God has called you to a task!
what_now_2: Psalm 32 is a call to repentance. Is there unconfessed sin in your life you are keeping from God? Turn it over to Him today!

label: August 13
what_now_1: Do some of your actions cause others to stumble?
what_now_2: Nehemiah made personal sacrifices to see the work on the wall completed. What is God calling you to sacrifice to advance His kingdom?

label: August 14
what_now_1: The Word of God is always a prominent part of any revival of God. How are you allowing the Word to impact your life and actions?
what_now_2: We are tempted to place our hope in jobs, money, education, and the government. Spend a few minutes right now to give thanks to God, the only One worthy of our hope... we are secure in Him!

label: August 15
what_now_1: If you can't hear the cries of the hurting, ask God to tune your ears. If you hear them, ask God to give you the strength to serve.
what_now_2: God is faithful, draw strength and security from Him. He knows our limits and will either proportion temptation to our strength, or provide us with the strength to overcome. Be faithful to Him by relying on Him. (See 1 Corinthians 10:13)

label: August 16
what_now_1: It is easy to get lost in genealogical records, but this account tells the story of Israel's return from exile and the fulfillment of God's promise to His people. How does fulfillment in the past impact your faith today?
what_now_2: The Israelites honored those willing to enter the city. Sometimes the faith community needs individuals to make sacrifices... what sacrifices can you make for your faith family?

label: August 17
what_now_1: What would you need to do to make Christ the head of your life? Your marriage? Your family? Your church?
what_now_2: 'Follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ.' ~ 1 Corinthians 11:1 -- What needs to change in your life before you can say that with confidence to others?

label: August 18
what_now_1: Mordecai drew the ire of Haman because he refused to worship another man. When are you tempted to worship someone rather than the God of the universe?
what_now_2: God's name is not mentioned in the book of Esther but his fingerprints are revealed throughout the book. How is God working in the background of your life?

label: August 19
what_now_1: Esther's actions required boldness and faith. How can you encourage others who are exercising boldness and faith?
what_now_2: Esther was providentially placed in the king's palace to preserve the nation of Israel. What has God strategically positioned you to do that no one else can accomplish?

label: August 20
what_now_1: Mordecai was esteemed because he spoke up for the good of his people. How can you speak up for the good of others?
what_now_2: God is concerned with our ministry and motives. Allow the Holy Spirit to reveal your motivations in ministry.

label: August 21
what_now_1: Does it make you uncomfortable that God suggests Job to Satan? Would God suggest you as a faithful servant?
what_now_2: Do you worship God for the things that God has given you... or do you worship God because He is God?

label: August 22
what_now_1: Is God's law in your heart? More practically, how is God's law in your actions and attitudes?
what_now_2: In Paul's description of the church the believers brought something to worship, rather than expecting something from worship. How does this truth impact your approach to worship?

label: August 23
what_now_1: The book of Job is interesting in that the 'speeches' by Job's friends are later rebuked by God. As you read these speeches, consider how they misrepresent God.
what_now_2: Job's words are laced with faith-filled questioning. He doesn't doubt God; He is trying to understand God. Be sensitive in your conversations with individuals who are hurting. Sometimes the venom of bitterness masks the foundation of faith. Help them find their foundation.

label: August 24
what_now_1: What steps do you need to take in order to make God a critical advisor in your decision making process.
what_now_2: How is the company you keep influencing your character?

label: August 25
what_now_1: Make a list of God's wonders. Then, try to bring them up in your conversations today!
what_now_2: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong. Show love in everything you do. ~ 1 Corinthians 16:13-14 (CEV)

label: August 26
what_now_1: Paul viewed hardship as God's reminder for complete dependence. How is God reminding you that His grace is sufficient?
what_now_2: The dialogue between Job and his friends continues to escalate. At some point it seems that both sides begin to focus on 'proving their side.' When do you allow conversations to escalate because of personal pride? Ask God to keep your pride in check.

label: August 27
what_now_1: The Corinthians started doubting the Gospel because Paul was forced to change his travel plans. When do you allow your convictions in the gospel to be impacted by the actions of man?
what_now_2: Parents, how are you teaching your children to follow Jesus?

label: August 28
what_now_1: Are you bound by the shackles of debt? Covenant with God and ask for his guidance in escaping the stronghold of indebtedness.
what_now_2: How is the aroma of Christ projected in your life?

label: August 29
what_now_1: Just as we are to carry the aroma of Christ, we are also to display the glory of Christ. How do the people you interact with see Christ reflected in your words and deeds?
what_now_2: Job took a proactive stance against lust. (Job 31:1) Make a similar commitment today and seek God's strength to fulfill it.

label: August 30
what_now_1: Are you like Job thinking that God has treated you unfairly? When we consider our sinfulness, be reminded that God has not treated us as we should be treated, instead, He has given us grace. Thank Him today for the grace that you do not deserve.
what_now_2: If you find yourself frustrated by the trials of life, re-read 2 Corinthians 4:8-9 and commit it to memory. The promise of that verse provides hope on dark days!

label: August 31
what_now_1: God responds to Job's questions with questions. God is revealing to Job and to us that He is the only one with the right to ask questions and that He is the only one capable of answering all questions. What questions would God ask of you today?
what_now_2: For we walk by faith, not by sight. ~ 2 Corinthians 5:7 (KJV)

label: September 1
what_now_1: Job discovers that the glory of God silences the questions of man. Meditate on the majesty of God today.
what_now_2: If you are not compelled by Christ's love to share God's grace... what's holding you back?

label: September 2
what_now_1: The Corinthians seemed to turn a cold shoulder to Paul even while he labored for them in print and prayer. How can you show honor to those who minister to you?
what_now_2: If fear and doubt trouble you, commit Psalm 46:1-2 to memory. #fearnot,

label: September 3
what_now_1: Can you readily think of people in your life that support you? If so then give thanks to God, if not, ask God to provide you with strong Christian friends.
what_now_2: Are you striving for achievement and advancement to the detriment of your walk with God?

label: September 4
what_now_1: Worldly sorrow is inspired by Satan and drowns us in guilt. Godly sorrow is inspired by God and leads us to confession, repentance, and restored relationship. Which type of sorrow is at work in your life?
what_now_2: Make a list of all the things that you have memorized; phone numbers, sports statistics, names, addresses, etc... Now make a list of the excuses you use to not memorize Scripture. You can memorize the things that are important to you! Select a verse to memorize today.

label: September 5
what_now_1: Many presume that they can 'get right with God' tomorrow, are you putting something off in your walk with God?
what_now_2: We are inundated with information, but the Bible still offers the best wisdom for living! What voices are competing with the voice of God in your life?

label: September 6
what_now_1: Paul speaks fondly of Titus and the others being sent to Corinth, together they work for the sake of the gospel. Are you a fellow servant ministering and contributing to others in your church or are you a consumer waiting to be served?
what_now_2: Paul and those on the missionary journey with him strive to do the right thing in God's eyes and in the eyes of the Corinthians. When are you tempted to switch that priority around and concern yourself more with the opinion of man than with the opinion of God?

label: September 7
what_now_1: What sins do you need to confess before God today? Follow David's example of confession and experience the restored joy of salvation.
what_now_2: While Solomon's sheep/teeth comparison probably won't translate very well... how might you compliment and affirm someone close to you today?

label: September 8
what_now_1: Isaiah condemns the people for piling up offerings while living in disobedience. Are you using things like church attendance, giving, etc. to mask sin in your life?
what_now_2: We readily accept the fact that God will turn our scarlet sin to snow, but do we acknowledge that grace requires surrender and obedience? Are there area's of your life that you are hesitant to surrender to Him? What are you waiting for?

label: September 9
what_now_1: But I am not surprised! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light. So it is no wonder that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. In the end they will get the punishment their wicked deeds deserve. ~ 2 Corinthians 11:14-15 (NLT)
what_now_2: Paul rebuked the Corinthians in love! Sometimes the most pointed and necessary rebukes come from those who love us.

label: September 10
what_now_1: God hasn't stopped seeking those willing to answer His call... What is God calling you to do?
what_now_2: Isaiah reminds us that just because we answer God's call doesn't mean the results are guaranteed. If you are growing weary in obedience ask God to strengthen your resolve.

label: September 11
what_now_1: God did not remove Paul's 'thorn in the flesh.' Sometimes the struggles of life are a part of God's plan. Is He using trials and difficulties to shape your life today?
what_now_2: When you face difficulties, remember the message God provided Paul, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' (2 Corinthians 12:9)

label: September 12
what_now_1: God's judgment must always be viewed in light of God's deliverance. When have you experienced His judgment? When have you experienced His deliverance?
what_now_2: Isaiah prophecies of a day when the wolf and lamb will live in harmony (Isaiah 11:6). The promise of a new world order will be fulfilled when Christ reigns on the earth.

label: September 13
what_now_1: Paul instructs the Corinthians to aim for perfection. We cannot accept mediocrity in our Christian journey, have you lowered your expectations to conform to the world?
what_now_2: Isaiah 12:2 (KJV): Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust, and not be afraid: for the LORD JEHOVAH is my strength and my song; he also is become my salvation.

label: September 14
what_now_1: False teachers misled the Galatians with a gospel based on good works and righteousness attained by actions. What gospel are you following?
what_now_2: Perplexed by an ever-changing political scene, the people of Israel wondered if God was in control. Isaiah relays the hopeful message that He is! People of God can trust that God is in control, even during chaotic times and the chaos of world politics.

label: September 15
what_now_1: Paul describes a moment when he was forced to confront Peter's hypocrisy. If God is calling you to confront the sin of another believer, then learn from Paul's example. Speak to them face to face... and also be willing to accept the rebuke of the saint who might point out the error of your own ways!
what_now_2: Parents are given the responsibility of disciplining their children. Without boundaries children will drift listlessly through life like a ship without sails. Ask God for help in providing the proper discipline to allow children to discover the proper limits in life.

label: September 16
what_now_1: Reading the news of today, be thankful that God is not dependent on the rulers of the nations.
what_now_2: Isaiah describes the desperate attempts of Israel to defend the city of Jerusalem. While the people work feverishly, they fail to call on God. When have you attempted to fix things on your own? What can you learn from those situations?

label: September 17
what_now_1: Sin often looks appealing... Where are you tempted to exchange your future hope for temporary joy?
what_now_2: Ask God to lead you on the straight path of obedience. (Isaiah 26:7-8)

label: September 18
what_now_1: Israel was rebuked because their words and actions did not match... How do your actions line up with your words?
what_now_2: Several New Testament writer's reference the cornerstone alluded to by Isaiah (Isaiah 28:16); Christ is the cornerstone providing a sure foundation.

label: September 19
what_now_1: We are called to honor our parents regardless of our age... What can you do today to honor your parents?
what_now_2: God has provided freedom in Christ. Legalism mocks Christ's sacrifice and enslaves the free. Is the yoke of legalism encroaching on areas of your faith?

label: September 20
what_now_1: Hezekiah was faced with insurmountable odds, we see his response in tomorrow's reading. How do you handle insurmountable odds? What is your natural reaction?
what_now_2: Isaiah predicts that water will gush forth in the wilderness (Isaiah 35:6). Years after Isaiah Jesus came to the earth offering Living Water.

label: September 21
what_now_1: Hezekiah sought God in the midst of his daunting situation. Do you have insurmountable issues to take to the All-Powerful God?
what_now_2: Do you trust, and believe that God can provide victory over your enemies?

label: September 22
what_now_1: Are you overwhelmed? Don't give up hope! God promises to renew the strength of those who continue to trust in Him (Isaiah 40:31). Confess your feelings of being overwhelmed and trust God's promise of renewal.
what_now_2: Hezekiah is punished for his pride. Are you guilty of showing off or taking credit for what you have accomplished or accumulated?

label: September 23
what_now_1: The Israelites Isaiah addresses ignored God's warnings... How might God be trying to warn you?
what_now_2: Once we experience God's grace, we are to do good works (Ephesians 2:10). Good works do not earn salvation... they bear testimony of salvation. Ask God to help keep your perspective.

label: September 24
what_now_1: Think of your sin for a moment... now praise God that the blood of Christ has blotted it out (Isaiah 43:25)
what_now_2: What do you worship? Are you worshiping things made with your own hands? (Your career, marriage, wealth, etc.)

label: September 25
what_now_1: God led Israel through the refining fire of conquest and exile because He loved them. How is God leading you through difficulties in order to draw you back to Him?
what_now_2: Do you have difficultires speaking the truth in love? Ask God to help you season your speech with love.

label: September 26
what_now_1: How is your life different today than it was before you met Christ? How is it different today than it was last year?
what_now_2: Wisdom brings strength, and knowledge gives power. ~ Proverbs 24:5 (Contemporary English Version)

label: September 27
what_now_1: Do not fear the words of enemies or economists; fear God! Ask God to give you the faith to worship Him above all else.
what_now_2: Husbands, how does your love for your spouse reflect Christ's love for the church?

label: September 28
what_now_1: Meditate on the armor of God. Thank God for the protection His armor has provided in the past, is providing... and will provide.
what_now_2: How does Paul's revelation that your battle is not against flesh and blood impact your view of the people around you (Ephesians 6:12)?

label: September 29
what_now_1: Are you frustrated by a lack of spiritual growth in your life? Take heart with the reminder that God will complete the work that He has started. (Philippians 1:6)
what_now_2: We cannot expect God's response to our prayers when our lives are soaked with sin. Ask the Holy Spirit to reveal unconfessed sin.

label: September 30
what_now_1: At home or at church, do you complain and argue? (Philippians 2:14) These seemingly insignificant actions can create chaos in the church and home.
what_now_2: In what areas do you tend to operate out of selfish ambition? (Philippians 2:3) Follow the example of Christ and place others ahead of yourself.

label: October 1
what_now_1: Paul affirms Timothy's service to the gospel. Like Timothy, someone in your sphere of influence needs to know that they are appreciated. Who could you encourage today?
what_now_2: Paul instructs the believers in Philippi to rejoice. He is not advising that they pretend trials do not exist. He is encouraging them to find joy in Christ in spite of the external trials. Find joy in the Lord today.

label: October 2
what_now_1: Ask God to increase your desire to 'know Christ and the power of his resurrection.' (Philippians 3:10)
what_now_2: How do your actions reflect that you are a citizen of heaven? (Philippians 3:20)

label: October 3
what_now_1: Like Jeremiah, God knew you before you were born and provided you with the skills to complete the purpose for which you have been set apart for. How does God want to use you to advance His kingdom?
what_now_2: Evaluate your thoughts according to the admonition in Philippians 4:8 (NLT): 'And now, dear brothers and sisters, one final thing. Fix your thoughts on what is true, and honorable, and right, and pure, and lovely, and admirable. Think about things that are excellent and worthy of praise.'

label: October 4
what_now_1: Israel refused to take an introspective look at their sinful ways. Take a hard look at your actions in light of God's Holiness? While it might not be pleasant... it will be profitable.
what_now_2: Who can you pray for today that needs to be filled with the knowledge of God's will?

label: October 5
what_now_1: Israel refused to acknowledge God's rebuke. Are you ignoring warning signs from God in your life?
what_now_2: In what ways are you tempted to take God for granted? His grace? His provision? etc.

label: October 6
what_now_1: Paul eloquently describes Christ's triumph on the cross which allowed us to move from death in sin to life in Christ. Take a few minutes to contemplate the eternal signficance of the verses in Colossians 2:13-15.
what_now_2: Have you lost the ability to blush at sin? Ask God to help you see sin from His holy perspective.

label: October 7
what_now_1: The scribes handled the word of God falsely leading the nation to sin. (Jeremiah 8:8) Pray for wisdom so that you, and those who teach you, will accurately interpret God's Word.
what_now_2: 'Think about the things of heaven, not the things of earth.' ~ Colossians 3:2 (NLT)

label: October 8
what_now_1: Paul requested prayer for his ministry. (Colossians 4:3-4) Who needs your prayer support today?
what_now_2: Ask God to fill your conversations with grace.

label: October 9
what_now_1: Are you like Israel, frequently wandering from God's path? What changes do you need to make in your life to still your wandering soul?
what_now_2: When you pray for God's intervention, is your prayer based on your needs or for God's glory? Our motivation for everything should be to glorify God! Psalm 79:9 (NLT): 'Help us, O God of our salvation! Help us for the glory of your name. Save us and forgive our sins for the honor of your name.

label: October 10
what_now_1: Paul encouraged, comforted and urged the Thessalonians to 'live lives worthy of God.' (1 Thessalonians 2:12) Who do you know that needs to be encouraged, comforted, and urged to live a life worthy of God?
what_now_2: Pray that God would allow your love for Him to overflow into the lives of others.

label: October 11
what_now_1: 'God's will is for you to be holy, so stay away from all sexual sin. Then each of you will control his own body and live in holiness and honor' ~ 1 Thessalonians 4:3-4 (NLT)
what_now_2: Your sin is as obvious to God as the sin of Israel denounced by Jeremiah. What do you need to confess that God already knows?

label: October 12
what_now_1: When was the last time you showed appreciation to those in a position of spiritual leadership? Send your pastor, minister, or small group leader a note or card expressing how important they are to you.
what_now_2: What is God doing in the world around you? Where is He inviting you to join Him?

label: October 13
what_now_1: While Jeremiah provided accurate messages from God, other false prophets distorted God's Word. False prophets still twist God's Word. How do you recognize that a message deviates from God's word?
what_now_2: The church in Thessalonica modeled perseverance in persecution. When you struggle, pray that God will grant you the strength to persevere with faithfulness.

label: October 14
what_now_1: 'Patient persuasion can break down the strongest resistance and can even convince rulers.' ~ Proverbs 25:15 (GNT)
what_now_2: Ponder this incredible truth today. How does it impact your understanding of God? 'I am everywhere-both near and far, in heaven and on earth. There are no secret places where you can hide from me.' ~ Jeremiah 23:23-24 (CEV)

label: October 15
what_now_1: Some pastors and teachers tell you what you want to hear while others tell you what you need to hear? Which do you think is more profitable?
what_now_2: Pray specifically and consistently for the spread of the gospel. (2 Thessalonians 3:1)

label: October 16
what_now_1: Jeremiah's letter was an encouragement to the exiles to continue living faithfully. How does turmoil threaten to draw you away from your faith? Continue trusting God's plan and purpose.
what_now_2: God used Paul's sinful past as a testimony to His transforming grace (1 Timothy 1:16). How is God being glorified through your past?

label: October 17
what_now_1: Paul did not want women to draw attention to themselves in their dress (1 Timothy 2:9-10). In what ways might you be a distraction to God for others by drawing attention to yourself?
what_now_2: Do you pray for our elected officials? The president? The governor? The mayor? You may not agree with their politics but they can all use your prayers.

label: October 18
what_now_1: Even though Judah was about to fall Jeremiah purchases a piece of land (Jeremiah 32:7), the prophet trusted in God's assurance that His people would one day repopulate the land. Do you find it difficult to trust God in times of despair? Know that God is still on His throne!
what_now_2: How can you show kindness to an enemy today?

label: October 19
what_now_1: God promises that He will reveal great things to those who call on Him. What do you expect when you call to God in prayer?
what_now_2: God's promises are as certain as the rising and setting of the sun (Jeremiah 33:19-22). Watch the sun set today and thank God for the consistency of His promises.

label: October 20
what_now_1: Do you toot your own horn to seek honor for yourself? (Proverbs 25:27)
what_now_2: Jehoiakim rejected God's commands by literally burning up the word of the Lord. Which areas of Scripture do you avoid because they make you uncomfortable?

label: October 21
what_now_1: The king ignored the word of God, yet still sought the Lord's favor. In what ways do you do the same?
what_now_2: If you find yourself in a pit today, trust that God will send a rescuer. If you are not in a pit... go rescue someone who is!

label: October 22
what_now_1: How are you at protecting that which God has entrusted to you?
what_now_2: After Israel fell, the remnant endured a tumultuous period of chaos. The absence of leadership often causes chaos. How can you be a calming influence during times of instability?

label: October 23
what_now_1: The Israelites trusted in the protection of Egypt over the protection of God. Are you trusting someone or something else for your protection?
what_now_2: Godless chatter is an infection that can kill the church. Are you guilty of spreading infection?

label: October 24
what_now_1: There is a difference between standing for Truth and picking fights...how can you stand for Truth without being quarrelsome?
what_now_2: All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we are wrong and teaches us to do what is right. ~ 2 Timothy 3:16 (NLT)

label: October 25
what_now_1: Nations that reject God might prosper for a period of time, but their sin does not escape the eyes of God.
what_now_2: Do you find yourself repeating the same mistakes? Ask God for wisdom.

label: October 26
what_now_1: Though God used Babylon to punish Israel, He promises punishment on Babylon as well. If circumstances seem unfair remember that God's justice will ultimately be fulfilled. Ask God to help you trust His timing.
what_now_2: God promises to forgive and restore Israel. Think about the restoration God has accomplished in your life. Praise Him for bringing you out of exile!

label: October 27
what_now_1: Which worldly passions do you have difficulty saying 'no' to?
what_now_2: Babylon was a superpower when Jeremiah proclaimed judgment on it. The Lord's judgment ultimately prevailed. Remember that God's power is far greater than any nations!

label: October 28
what_now_1: The description of people and property taken into exile is a sad reminder of the consequences of sin. What consequences are you risking by continuing in sin?
what_now_2: The sinking scroll must have lifted the spirits of the Israelites in Babylon (Jeremiah 51:64). Remember that whatever troubles you face today are 'light and momentary' (2 Corinthians 4:17). Just as God rescued Israel from exile, He will rescue you!

label: October 29
what_now_1: The forlorn lament of Jeremiah reminds us of the emptiness of sin. Think about a time when you recognized the emptiness of sin. Are you engaged in activities today that will lead you to the same desperate emptiness?
what_now_2: Paul makes a bold appeal on behalf of a slave, Onesimus. Who needs your bold voice today? Remember, your silence perpetuates injustice.

label: October 30
what_now_1: God speaks through His Word just as He spoke through the burning bush. How does the knowledge that God has spoken to you impact your understanding of God? Of the Bible?
what_now_2: By faith we can always count on Gods ear and grace to quiet our fear.

label: October 31
what_now_1: How are you encouraged knowing that Jesus was tempted as you are tempted?
what_now_2: The physical destruction of Israel in the Bible mirrored their spiritual decay. Be in prayer today for the spiritual state of your nation.

label: November 1
what_now_1: Ask God to protect you from the deceitful speech that surrounds you.
what_now_2: Do not be afraid to speak the truth, or be put off by the words of those that would hinder you. In uncompromising service to God, speak and act boldly with assured protection from above.

label: November 2
what_now_1: Are you allowing God's Word to penetrate your life or are you simply allowing the Word to skim the surface? How can you let the Word rearrange your life!
what_now_2: Are you guilty of ever seeking revenge? Have you ever considered that the person is likely to have already forgotten the incident? Ask God to help you forgive and forget.

label: November 3
what_now_1: How have you grown in your faith? In what areas do you need to mature?
what_now_2: Ask God to help you see sin as He sees sin.

label: November 4
what_now_1: Are you in need of a heart transplant? Seek an undivided heart for God! (Ezekiel 11:19)
what_now_2: In what areas of your life are you producing abundant crops? In what areas of your life are you producing thorns and thistles? (Hebrews 6:7-8)

label: November 5
what_now_1: Do you allow horoscopes, psychics and other pagan rituals to deceive you? While they might appear harmless they divert our attention from God.
what_now_2: The prophets of Israel are rebuked for giving a flimsy, false message that was coated in flowering words. Are you tempted to believe flowery words, and miss God's leadership? (Ezekiel 13:8-12)

label: November 6
what_now_1: Ezekiel 16 mirrors our story, like Israel, we were helpless and hopeless. Do you remember your hopelessness without Christ? Take a few moments to reflect on God's grace.
what_now_2: Praise the Lord! Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good! His faithful love endures forever. ~ Psalm 106:1 (NLT)

label: November 7
what_now_1: Sodom was arrogant, overfed and unconcerned. Could those same terms be used to describe you? Pray that God would start a revival in you that would pour forth to others.
what_now_2: God is greater than any nation, He brings nations to power and allows them to fall. Pray for God's will to be accomplished through the nations of this world.

label: November 8
what_now_1: What steps can you take to transform your faith from expressions of external religion... to acts of internal devotion?
what_now_2: Ask God to tune your heart to match His heart concerning the wicked and disobedient.

label: November 9
what_now_1: The sacrifice for our sin was accomplished forever through Christ. Are you denying Christ's sacrifice by continually attempting to earn salvation?
what_now_2: 'For by the power of the eternal Spirit, Christ offered himself to God as a perfect sacrifice for our sins.' ~ Hebrews 9:14b (NLT)

label: November 10
what_now_1: What can you do to increase your understanding of God's expectations revealed in His law?
what_now_2: In response to Christ's sacrifice we are called to give ourselves. How are you giving yourself to the cause of Christ?

label: November 11
what_now_1: Even after God delivered Israel from Egypt they continued to practice the idolatry committed in Egypt. Are there idols in your life you are still worshipping?
what_now_2: God is too big to be hidden behind your back. When do you attempt to exclude God from the activities of your life?

label: November 12
what_now_1: Sin does not go unnoticed by God. Are there 'secret' sins in your life that you are pretending God doesn't see? Take time to confess these sins.
what_now_2: Are there 'faith heroes' in your life? Why not encourage them with a card or call today.

label: November 13
what_now_1: God speaks against the arrogance of Tyre. The nation assumed that it's strength would keep it safe. Spend some time in prayer today specifically for your country.
what_now_2: In today's reading in Hebrews, Abraham trusted God, the Promiser, more than he trusted the Promise. How can you differentiate between trusting the Promiser and trusting the Promise?

label: November 14
what_now_1: How is God disciplining you? How are you accepting His discipline?
what_now_2: Israel considered Egypt their constant safety net. They always assumed that Egypt would bail them out in times of trouble. Is there an Egypt in your life? Who do you believe can save you from difficult times?

label: November 15
what_now_1: When have you exchanged a blessing for a bowl of stew? (Hebrews 12:16-17) How might you be tempted to place the temporary things of this earth ahead of the eternal things of God?
what_now_2: World powers come and go. Remember that the only eternal kingdom is the one initiated by Christ. How does your life bear testimony to your citizenship in God's kingdom?

label: November 16
what_now_1: Like Ezekiel, you have been entrusted as a watchman (Ezekiel 33:7). Is there someone in your circle that needs to hear a warning from you?
what_now_2: 'Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.' ~ Hebrews 13:8 (NLT)

label: November 17
what_now_1: God did not restore Israel based on their goodness or righteousness. He restored Israel for the sake of His name. Do you ever assume that God saved you for your inherent value?
what_now_2: Every good and perfect gift is from above... (James 1:17). Take a few moments to thank God for all He has done for you. "

label: November 18
what_now_1: Are you quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry? (James 1:19) It seems that most of us have that Biblical mandate reversed. Be diligent this week to listen closely and avoid anger.
what_now_2: ...faith by itself isn't enough. Unless it produces good deeds, it is dead and useless. (James 2:17) How does this reality impact your understanding of faith?

label: November 19
what_now_1: How can you serve as a peacemaker in your sphere of influence?
what_now_2: If we are on God's side we need not be afraid, He is with us. If God is with us then, who can be against us!

label: November 20
what_now_1: Ezekiel's vision of the Temple was a vision of hope. Though the Temple was in ruins, God gave Ezekiel a vision of the Temple restored. How is God giving you a vision of a restored life, family or church?
what_now_2: So humble yourselves before God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. ~ James 4:7 (NLT)

label: November 21
what_now_1: Are you using the material resources God has provided you with to assist others in need?
what_now_2: Try to envision God filling the Temple with His glory as described in Ezekiel 43:5. Are you tempted to be so familiar with God that you lose sight of His splendor?

label: November 22
what_now_1: How does it make you feel to know that you have been chosen by God?
what_now_2: Ezekiel is overwhelmed by the glory of God. When was the last time you were overhwhelmed by God's glory?

label: November 23
what_now_1: 'So think clearly and exercise self-control. Look forward to the gracious salvation that will come to you when Jesus Christ is revealed to the world.' ~ 1 Peter 1:13 (NLT)
what_now_2: 'So get rid of all evil behavior. Be done with all deceit, hypocrisy, jealousy, and all unkind speech.' ~ 1 Peter 2:1 (NLT)

label: November 24
what_now_1: Do you have trouble accepting authority? Remember that your attitude toward authority reveals your attitude toward God.
what_now_2: Do you spend as much time on your inner beauty as you do on your outer beauty? In what ways can you do more to cultivate your inner beauty?

label: November 25
what_now_1: Daniel displayed tact and wisdom. How do your words display tact? How do your lips provide wisdom?
what_now_2: Do not be afraid of your enemies. Your enemies are God's enemies, and they can do nothing to you unless allowed by His permission. Instead sanctify the Lord in your hearts through sincere reverence, trusting his faithfulness, submitting to His wisdom and giving Him glory.

label: November 26
what_now_1: Daniel credited God with his ability to interpret the dream. (Daniel 2:45) Are you ever tempted to take credit for God's activity in your life?
what_now_2: Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego trusted that God could save them but were willing to stand boldy for God even if He did not save them. Pray that God would strengthen His people, especially those who are being persecuted for their faith.

label: November 27
what_now_1: How is your approach to the Bible changed when you consider that Scripture is not the opinion of man, but the Word of God?
what_now_2: Nebuchadnezzar sent a letter to his people proclaiming God's activity in his life. Make an announcement today applauding God's activity in your own life. Call a friend, post a message or send an email.

label: November 28
what_now_1: Daniel was often forced to convey difficult messages. Pray that God would provide you with boldness when you are called on to share challenging messages.
what_now_2: How can you recognize false prophets? How do you know if a message or messenger is from God or not?

label: November 29
what_now_1: Daniel's enemies could find no fault in his work. Ask God to help you display integrity at work so that you can glorify Him.
what_now_2: In the midst of trouble and worry we find comfort in the word of God.

label: November 30
what_now_1: Who has helped you by providing constructive criticism instead of flattery? Contact one of them today to thank them.
what_now_2: John wrote to give testimony of Christ. Who needs to hear a testimony about Jesus from your lips? Pray that God provides you with the opportunity and courage to testify for Christ.

label: December 1
what_now_1: How does the love of the world threaten to distract you in your walk with Christ? What precautions can you take to avoid these distractions?
what_now_2: To walk as Jesus walked is an impossible task in our own strength. Ask God to give you strength and wisdom as you seek to follow Jesus with your life.

label: December 2
what_now_1: Acknowledge God and give Him Praise for how He has helped you through this past week.
what_now_2: We are assured that our prayers are heard when offered earnestly and with humility, and are expectant and unafraid of His answer. Just as God's word is enlightening to us, know that our prayers are pleasing to Him. #fearnot

label: December 3
what_now_1: Ask God to reveal steps you can take in order to show God's love to those around you.

label: December 4
what_now_1: How do you test the messages you hear to discern if they are from God?
what_now_2: God is in control of the end just as He was in control of the beginning. Meditate on the eternity that God promises to those who follow Him.

label: December 5
what_now_1: What idols are distracting you from Christ?
what_now_2: It's easy to downplay the effects of sin, but God compares Israel's sinfulness and idolatry to adultery. How does this comparison change your perception of sin?

label: December 6
what_now_1: Hosea addresses situations that look eerily familiar to our contemporary culture. Pray for Gods intervention locally, nationally and globally, and to reveal ways that you can be more engaged for the Gospel.
what_now_2: The actions of Israel prohibited them from worshiping God. Ask God to expose the sinful behavior in your life that might be keeping you from connecting with God.

label: December 7
what_now_1: What can you do to acknowledge God outside the walls of the church?
what_now_2: The expression 'You reap what you sow.' is derived from Hosea 8:7. What type of harvest can you expect from your current course of action?

label: December 8
what_now_1: Israel's idolatry revealed that they had forgotten the One who delivered them, what can you do to be reminded of the deliverance you have through Christ?
what_now_2: God wanted to bless the nations through Israel but instead they focused inwardly. Christians are tempted the same today, how can you reveal God's blessing by blessing others?

label: December 9
what_now_1: Even as God warns Israel of impending judgment, He extends grace. Who do you know that needs to hear about God's grace? Share with them about the God who is 'gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in love.' (Joel 2:13)
what_now_2: 'Look, he is coming...' (Revelation 1:7). How DOES the imminent return of Christ impact your life? How SHOULD the imminent return of Christ impact your life?

label: December 10
what_now_1: Amos proclaimed judgment on the surrounding nations, he then announced God's judgment on Israel. It's always easier to see sin in the lives of others, ask God to make your sin apparent so you can confess them and receive His forgiveness.
what_now_2: The message to the church in Smyrna is 'Be faithful' and 'Do not be afraid.' (Revelation 2:10). Perhaps you are facing difficult times and in need of the same exhortation.

label: December 11
what_now_1: Israel had slipped into an attitude of entitlement and complacency. What has God placed on your heart in order to get more involved with His mission? Ask Him to help you overcome any complacency in your life.
what_now_2: Some at the church in Sardis were coasting in their faith. Are their areas of your faith that are decaying from a lack of exercise? What can you do to stimulate growth in these areas?

label: December 12
what_now_1: Jesus offers to dine with His people. The meal was considered a time of fellowship and relationship building. Plan a time this week when you can have an extended time of prayer, seeking to cultivate your relationship with Christ.
what_now_2: Amos was a shepherd by vocation, not a prophet. What is God calling you to do that might be outside your comfort zone?

label: December 13
what_now_1: Do you worry about the opinions of others? Ask the Holy Spirit to direct you to seek and trust God.
what_now_2: Does your Bible reading leave you wondering What Now? The #OYB takes on new depth with this wonderful companion guide.

label: December 14
what_now_1: God wants to use us to extend grace just like he used Jonah. How is He using you?
what_now_2: 'Worthy is the lamb.' ~ Revelation 5:12 -- Consider sharing why Jesus is worthy of your praise with someone today!

label: December 15
what_now_1: The people didn't believe Michah's warning of impending judgment while they were experiencing economic prosperity. External circumstances are not always accurate indicators of an internal spiritual condition. Take time today to do an honest assessment of your spiritual health.
what_now_2: Look for situations that reveal that God is in control throughout the day to be reminded of His dominion.

label: December 16
what_now_1: God desires for His people to walk humbly with Him. How is your walk? Are there things you could change to strengthen that walk?
what_now_2: Every word of God proves true. he is a shield to all who come to him for protection. ~ Proverbs 30:5 NLT

label: December 17
what_now_1: Is there sin in your life that you've grown comfortable with that God is calling you to confess?
what_now_2: Make a list of those times in your life when God's power was evident. After each item record the reminder of the Psalmist, 'His love endures forever.'

label: December 18
what_now_1: 'The Sovereign Lord is my strength! He makes me as surefooted as a deer, able to tread upon the heights.' ~ Habakkuk 3:19 (NLT)
what_now_2: Are you struggling with the silence of God? Find strength in knowing that He is still in control, share your concerns with Him today to reveal that you are not afraid of His silence.

label: December 19
what_now_1: Zephaniah promises that the 'day of the LORD' is coming... Are you ready?
what_now_2: John was called to share the message he received on Patmos, what is God calling you to share with your friends and family... hope? encouragement? warning?

label: December 20
what_now_1: Today's Psalm reminds us that God knows our thoughts. Do they glorify Him? Do they dishonor Him? Ask Him to help you take your thoughts captive!
what_now_2: Israel delayed rebuilding the Temple claiming that the time wasn't right... are you being tempted to hold back what rightfully belongs to God?

label: December 21
what_now_1: God's message through Zechariah promises hope. God continues to spread hope to His people, ask Him to rekindle yours.

label: December 22
what_now_1: Then I pray to you, O Lord. I say, 'You are my place of refuge. You are all I really want in life.' ~ Psalm 142:5 NLT
what_now_2: The Psalmist seeks the Lord's presence through prayer. Do you seek His presence in your prayer life? Are there things in your life that could be changed to make your prayer life more personal?

label: December 23
what_now_1: God assured Zechariah that only by the Lord's power would the temple be rebuilt. Are you attempting things that can only be accomplished by God?
what_now_2: Silent Night was first sung almost 200 years ago on Christmas Eve, 1818, in the little village church at Oberndorf in Austria. Father Joseph Mohr, a priest, wrote the original lyrics along with Franz Gruber, a teacher, composing the music. The lyrics best known to us today were written in 1859. The version here is a beautiful saxaphone solo performed by Hamilton Hardin at a previous Christmas Eve service. Wishing you a blessed Christmas!

label: December 24
what_now_1: Make Davids prayer your prayer: 'Teach me to do your will, for you are my God.' ~ Psalm 143:10a

label: December 25
what_now_1: Zechariah promises a time of revival and renewal in Israel. Pray that God send revival and renewal to your church and community.

label: December 26
what_now_1: Let Psalm 145 be your prayer, 'I will praise you, my God and King, and always honor your name. I will praise you each day and always honor your name. You are wonderful, LORD, and you deserve all praise, because you are much greater than anyone can understand...'

label: December 27
what_now_1: Todays reading in Zecharia talks of the Lord restoring His people... spend time today to thank God for the areas of your life He has restored, then submit to Him those areas that are still in need of His restoring touch.
what_now_2: The Bible In A Year reading plan takes on new depth with this wonderful companion guide, perfect for personal or small group study: http://amzn.to/rNOrE3

label: December 28
what_now_1: Do you praise Jesus as a victorious conqueror like heaven praises the Lamb in today's reading from Revelation? Give praise to the One that has conquered death.
what_now_2: Are you planning to read through the Bible this next year? Join us on this spiritual journey at Bible In A Year Online Page and be sure to encourage your Facebook friends by 'Sharing' this post with them.

label: December 29
what_now_1: If God were physically ruling on the earth today, how would your life change?
what_now_2: Let them praise the name of the Lord: for his name alone is excellent; his glory is above the earth and heaven. ~ Psalm 148:13

label: December 30
what_now_1: Malachi writes of 'unworthy sacrifices', are you giving your best to God or are there areas in your life where you are just giving Him your leftovers?
what_now_2: When we are equipped with the word of God we are effectively prepared to face the chill of fear during life's storms. #fearnot

label: December 31
what_now_1: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to give you this message for the churches. I am both the source of David and the heir to his throne. I am the bright morning star. The Spirit and the bride say, 'Come.' Let anyone who hears this say, 'Come.' Let anyone who is thirsty come. Let anyone who desires drink freely from the water of life. ~ Revelation 22:16-17 (NLT) -- Please join us through the Bible this year! Remember that when you are reading the Bible, you are not just reading some stories, you are reading God's word... God is speaking to you!
what_now_2: Congratulations! Click Like or Share if you are completing your read through the Bible in a year today! -- I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, and I have remained faithful. ~ 2 Timothy 4:7 -- Be encouraged knowing that people from over 200 countries have joined us.
